xxxx-17 1/9/2018  Susan McCartney   Karen Carnevale Council President   Municipal Clerk RESOLUTION-CASH MANAGEMENT PLAN  WHEREAS, N.J.S.A. 40A:5-14 mandates that the Governing Body of a municipal corporation shall, by resolution passed by a majority vote of the full membership thereof, designate as a depository for its monies a bank or trust company having its place of business in the state and organized under the laws of the United States or this state; and WHEREAS, N.J.S.A. 40A:5-15.1 amended by Chapter 148, P.L. 1997 established new requirements for the investment of public funds and adoption of a cash management plan for counties, municipalities and authorities; and WHEREAS, the Mayor and Township Council of the Township of West Orange wish to comply with the above statutes; NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED, that the Mayor and Township Council of the Township of West Orange adopt the attached cash management plan, including the official depositories for the Township of West Orange for the 2018 year.

12 TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE EMERGENCY TEMPORARY APPROPRIATION RESOLUTION FOR THE PURPOSE OF FUNDING EMINENT TAX APPEALS PRIOR TO ADOPTION OF 2018 BUDGET -N.J.S. 40A:4-20  WHEREAS, an emergent condition has arisen with respect to the continued operation of the Municipality, and more apporpriations are needed to pay tax appeal claims, and no adequate provision has been made in the 2018 Temporary Budget for the aforesaid purpose, and N.J.S.40A:4-20 provides for the creation of an emergency temporary appropriation for the purpose above mentioned, and NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED, (not less than two-thirds of all members, thereof affirmatively concurring) that in accordance with N.J.S. 40A:4-20, emergency temporary appropriations be and the same are hereby made for the purpose indentified by the titles herein listed and the sums set opposite such titles:  2018 EMERGENCY TEMPORARY APPROPRIATIONS ARE HEREBY ATTACHED AND MADE PART OF THIS RESOLUTION Tax Appeals   500,000.00  Karen Carnevale   Susan McCartney Municipal Clerk   Council President

xxxx-17 1/9/2018  Joe Krakoviak   Karen Carnevale Council President   Municipal Clerk RESOLUTION-CASH MANAGEMENT PLAN  WHEREAS, N.J.S.A. 40A:5-14 mandates that the Governing Body of a municipal corporation shall, by resolution passed by a majority vote of the full membership thereof, designate as a depository for its monies a bank or trust company having its place of business in the state and organized under the laws of the United States or this state; and WHEREAS, N.J.S.A. 40A:5-15.1 amended by Chapter 148, P.L. 1997 established new requirements for the investment of public funds and adoption of a cash management plan for counties, municipalities and authorities; and WHEREAS, the Mayor and Township Council of the Township of West Orange wish to comply with the above statutes; NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED, that the Mayor and Township Council of the Township of West Orange adopt the attached cash management plan, including the official depositories for the Township of West Orange for the 2018 year.

12 TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE EMERGENCY TEMPORARY APPROPRIATION RESOLUTION FOR THE PURPOSE OF FUNDING EMINENT TAX APPEALS PRIOR TO ADOPTION OF 2018 BUDGET -N.J.S. 40A:4-20  WHEREAS, an emergent condition has arisen with respect to the continued operation of the Municipality, and more apporpriations are needed to pay tax appeal claims, and no adequate provision has been made in the 2018 Temporary Budget for the aforesaid purpose, and N.J.S.40A:4-20 provides for the creation of an emergency temporary appropriation for the purpose above mentioned, and NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED, (not less than two-thirds of all members, thereof affirmatively concurring) that in accordance with N.J.S. 40A:4-20, emergency temporary appropriations be and the same are hereby made for the purpose indentified by the titles herein listed and the sums set opposite such titles:  2018 EMERGENCY TEMPORARY APPROPRIATIONS ARE HEREBY ATTACHED AND MADE PART OF THIS RESOLUTION Tax Appeals   500,000.00  Karen Carnevale   Joe Krakoviak Municipal Clerk   Council President

xxxx-17 1/9/2018  Joe Krakoviak   Karen Carnevale Council President   Municipal Clerk RESOLUTION-CASH MANAGEMENT PLAN  WHEREAS, N.J.S.A. 40A:5-14 mandates that the Governing Body of a municipal corporation shall, by resolution passed by a majority vote of the full membership thereof, designate as a depository for its monies a bank or trust company having its place of business in the state and organized under the laws of the United States or this state; and WHEREAS, N.J.S.A. 40A:5-15.1 amended by Chapter 148, P.L. 1997 established new requirements for the investment of public funds and adoption of a cash management plan for counties, municipalities and authorities; and WHEREAS, the Mayor and Township Council of the Township of West Orange wish to comply with the above statutes; NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED, that the Mayor and Township Council of the Township of West Orange adopt the attached cash management plan, including the official depositories for the Township of West Orange for the 2018 year.

12 TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE EMERGENCY TEMPORARY APPROPRIATION RESOLUTION FOR THE PURPOSE OF FUNDING EMINENT TAX APPEALS PRIOR TO ADOPTION OF 2018 BUDGET -N.J.S. 40A:4-20  WHEREAS, an emergent condition has arisen with respect to the continued operation of the Municipality, and more apporpriations are needed to pay tax appeal claims, and no adequate provision has been made in the 2018 Temporary Budget for the aforesaid purpose, and N.J.S.40A:4-20 provides for the creation of an emergency temporary appropriation for the purpose above mentioned, and NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED, (not less than two-thirds of all members, thereof affirmatively concurring) that in accordance with N.J.S. 40A:4-20, emergency temporary appropriations be and the same are hereby made for the purpose indentified by the titles herein listed and the sums set opposite such titles:  2018 EMERGENCY TEMPORARY APPROPRIATIONS ARE HEREBY ATTACHED AND MADE PART OF THIS RESOLUTION Tax Appeals   500,000.00  Karen Carnevale   Joe Krakoviak Municipal Clerk   Council President

19-18 1/9/2018  Joe Krakoviak   Karen Carnevale Council President   Municipal Clerk RESOLUTION-CASH MANAGEMENT PLAN  WHEREAS, N.J.S.A. 40A:5-14 mandates that the Governing Body of a municipal corporation shall, by resolution passed by a majority vote of the full membership thereof, designate as a depository for its monies a bank or trust company having its place of business in the state and organized under the laws of the United States or this state; and WHEREAS, N.J.S.A. 40A:5-15.1 amended by Chapter 148, P.L. 1997 established new requirements for the investment of public funds and adoption of a cash management plan for counties, municipalities and authorities; and WHEREAS, the Mayor and Township Council of the Township of West Orange wish to comply with the above statutes; NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED, that the Mayor and Township Council of the Township of West Orange adopt the attached cash management plan, including the official depositories for the Township of West Orange for the 2018 year.

12 TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE EMERGENCY TEMPORARY APPROPRIATION RESOLUTION FOR THE PURPOSE OF FUNDING EMINENT TAX APPEALS PRIOR TO ADOPTION OF 2018 BUDGET -N.J.S. 40A:4-20  WHEREAS, an emergent condition has arisen with respect to the continued operation of the Municipality, and more apporpriations are needed to pay tax appeal claims, and no adequate provision has been made in the 2018 Temporary Budget for the aforesaid purpose, and N.J.S.40A:4-20 provides for the creation of an emergency temporary appropriation for the purpose above mentioned, and NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED, (not less than two-thirds of all members, thereof affirmatively concurring) that in accordance with N.J.S. 40A:4-20, emergency temporary appropriations be and the same are hereby made for the purpose indentified by the titles herein listed and the sums set opposite such titles:  2018 EMERGENCY TEMPORARY APPROPRIATIONS ARE HEREBY ATTACHED AND MADE PART OF THIS RESOLUTION Tax Appeals   500,000.00  Karen Carnevale   Joe Krakoviak Municipal Clerk   Council President

246 -17  December 5, 2017  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Essex   County   Division   of   Senior   Services   has   agreed   to   provide  partial   funding   for   a   Senior   Citizen   Public   Health   Nurse   for   the   contract   period   of:   (i)  January 1,   2018   through December 31,   2018 , in the amount of $16,127;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Health   Officer   has   reviewed   the   annexed   agreement   and  recommends execution and approval   of the contract ; and  WHEREAS,   the Township has participated in this program in previous years; and  WHEREAS,   annexed hereto is a copy of the   proposed   agreement;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the   annexed  agreement   with   the   Essex   County   Division   of   Senior   Services   for   the   time   periods  specified   herein,   in   connection   with   the   partial   funding   of   a   Senior   Citizen   Public   Health  Nurse ,   and   the   Municipal   Clerk   shall   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   attest   to   the   Mayor ’ s  signature; and it is further  RESOLVED,   that   a   permanent   copy   of   this   agreement   shall   be   maintained   by   the  Municipal Clerk and the Department of Health.  Karen J. C arnevale , R.M.C.   Joe Krakoviak  Municipal   Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   December 5, 2017

documents.title: AUTHORIZING AGREEMENT WITH BENECARD FOR PRESCRIPTION DRUGS - 2018

pages.text: 247-17  December 5, 2017  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   on   or   about   September   17,   2013,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Council  authorized   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   to   enter   into   a   Contract   for   Employee  Prescription Drug Insurance (the   “ Agreement ” ) with Benecard Services, Inc. ( “ Benecard ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Agreement   was   thereafter   renewed   for   a   term   from   November   1 ,   2014  through   October   31,   2015   pursuant   to   the   First   Amendment   to   Agreement   to   Provide   Fixed   Rate  Insured Prescription Drug Management Services, annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A; ”   and  WHEREAS,   the   Agreement   was   thereafter   renewed   for   a   term   from   November   1,   2015  through   December   31,   2016   pursuant   to   the   terms   set   forth   in   the   renewal   letter   dated   October  20, 2015, annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ B; ”   and  WHEREAS,   the   Agreement   was   thereafter   renewed   again   for   a   term   from   January   1,  2017 through December 31, 2017 based on the rate charts annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ C ; ”   and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   now   seeks   to   renew   the   Agreement   for   a n   additional   term  from   January   1, 201 8   through December 31, 201 8   (the   “ Renewal ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the   rates   and   costs   of   the   Renewal   are   set   forth   in   the   r enewal   letter   dated  November 20, 2017 (the   “ Renewal Letter ” )   annexed hereto as   Exhibit   “ D . ”  WHEREAS ,   the   Mayor   has   recused   himself   from   the   renewal   of   the   Agreement,  including the execution of any documents related to the renewal of the Agreement.  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   shall   and   hereby   does   authorize   the  Renewal ,   consistent   with   the   rates   set   forth   in   the   R enewal   Letter   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit  “ D ” ; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Business   Administrator   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   all  documents necessary to effectuate   the Renewal ; and be it further

RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   resolution   shall   be   published   and   made   available   in   the  Clerk ’ s Office for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J.   Carnevale , R.M.C. Benecard Services, LLC • 3131 Princeton Pike • Building 2B, Suite 103 • Lawrenceville, NJ 08648 (609) 219-0400 • Fax (609) 219-1788 • www.benecard.com  November 20, 2017  Mr. Ryan Graham Fairview Insurance Agency 25 Fairview Avenue Verona, NJ 07044 Re:   Benecard ®   Prescription Benefit Program   2018 Revised Renewal, Township of West Orange  Dear Ryan, Good news! Benecard has reviewed the prescription benefit program activities to date for the current plan year and is pleased to offer the following revised program renewal. The new program charges will become effective January 1, 2018 expiring December 31, 2018 and reflect an overall decrease of approximately 1 ½%. This offering does not include any services/fees for purposes of administering the Township of West Orange’s Retiree Drug Subsidy (RDS) program. This offering will remain valid for (60) days. The proposed renewal program charges provided below does not account for any Federal and/or State government programs, fees, taxes or regulations including, but not limited to, the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act with the exception of certain preventive health services and coverage for dependents to the age 26. The proposed renewal program charges also assume that the present benefit design, co- pay structure, and participation level will remain unchanged. Should any of these factors change during the contract period, Benecard reserves the right to revise these program charges. The proposed program charges include insurance carrier charges by Heartland Fidelity Insurance Company. These charges guarantee program costs by the carrier for the contract period. Benecard Services, LLC • 3131 Princeton Pike • Building 2B, Suite 103 • Lawrenceville, NJ 08648 (609) 219-0400 • Fax (609) 219-1788 • www.benecard.com  Page 2,   Benecard   ®   Prescription Benefit Program   2018 Revised Renewal, Township of West Orange Benecard Client ID#   1625   – Group #’s   7100/8100/9100  Retail Co-pay: $21 Preferred Brand / $9 Generic / $41 Non-Preferred Brand Retail Day Supply Limitations: 1-30 day supply 1x co-pay, 31-60 2x co-pay, 61-90 3x co-pay Mail Order Co-pay: $31 Preferred Brand / $10 Generic / $52 Non-Preferred Brand Mail Order Day Supply Limitation: up to 90 days 1 Out of Pocket Limit (separate): $1,222 for individual and $13,900 for family Census Coverage   Single   Parent/Child   Parent/Children   Member/Spouse   Family   Totals Current Census   131   9   2   126   71   339 Monthly Program Charges   294.97   722.04   722.04   589.90   1,120.82  Annualized Program Cost Based on Current Census   2,405,870 Over Age Dependent Program Charge for 1/1/2018 - 12/31/2018   $235.28  Should the proposed program charges change at any point during the 2018 contract period the overage dependent program charge will be changed accordingly.  1   The Affordable Care Act requires the Township of West Orange’s health benefits program implement an out-of-pocket (OOP) limit to protect individuals from excessive OOP expenses on all essential health benefits on or after January 1, 2015. The OOP limits represented above are based on the Township of West Orange’s request to have a separate prescription OOP and at these required dollar limits effective January 1, 2018. It is our understanding that the Township of West Orange’s acceptance of this proposal is based on their request to have a separate OOP limit combined with all other essential health benefit OOP limits that does not exceed the annual limitation on the OOP’s for that year as outlined by the Department of Labor. The Rx OOP limits are subject to change if the ACA OOP limits are modified. Benecard reserves the right to revise the Township of West Orange’s prescription benefit program charges if the client modifies or implements a different copayment level, coinsurance, deductible or any other benefit design change, including a change to the OOP amount that differs from what was used for underwriting purposes to develop for the program charges provided herein or enacts a change after inception of the new contract start date. We aggressively manage program costs for our clients while working within the confines of their benefit design. However, as you know, a key to successfully managing prescription benefit costs is implementing plan design and co-payment changes that control over-utilization and expenditures on higher priced medications. If the Township of West Orange is preparing for collective bargaining or budgeting costs, please do not hesitate to call on us to develop a customized benefit analysis. Benecard offers benefit modeling services to its clients in which we provide recommendations for a variety of plan modifications. Along with the recommendations, Benecard will identify both the potential program cost impact and the possible impact benefit changes could have on plan participants if implemented. Depending on existing benefit features and utilization patterns carefully planned benefit modifications can result in savings ranging from 5% to 25% of program costs. We work closely with our clients and their professional benefit consultants   to   deliver   a   benefit   plan   that   is   consistent   with   their   human   resource   and   budgetary objectives. We look forward to continuing to provide the highest quality of client and participant services during the coming plan year, and to continue our longstanding relationship based on quality of service and careful program cost management. Do feel free to contact me should any questions arise regarding this revised renewal proposal. Very truly yours, Barbara Seifert  Client Relations Manager cc: Kelly Monahan, Client Services Director Julie Graham, Fairview Insurance Agency

documents.title: AUTHORIZING EXECUTION OF REVISED SCOPE OF WORK ATTACHMENT FOR LIBRARY LIGHT UPGRADES

pages.text: 248-17  December 5, 2017  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   previously   entered   into   a  Participation   Agreement   for   the   New   Jersey   Clean   Energy   Program ’ s   Direct   Install   Program   (the  “ Program ” )   to   upgrade   the   lighting   at   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Public   Library   (the  “ Library ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Contractor   performing   the   installation   program   for   the   Township,   Willdan  Energy   Solutions   ( “ Wildan ” )   has   advised   that   the   requirements   of   the   Program   have   changed   to  remove the replacement of ballasts as part of the Program; and  WHEREAS,   the   current   existing   ballasts   cannot   support   the   new   LED   light   being  installed   and   must   therefore   be   added   to   the   Scope   of   Work   Attachment   to   the   Participation  Agreement; and  WHEREAS,   Wildan   has   advised   that   the   added   cost   of   replacing   the   ballasts   will  increase   the   cost   to   the   Township   by   $2,602.80   as   set   forth   in   the   revised   Scope   of   Work  Attachment, annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A. ”  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   shall   and   hereby   does   authorize   the  revised Scot of Work Attachment, annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A; ”   and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the   revised   Scot   of  Work Attachment, annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A; ”   and be it further

RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   Office  for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. pages.text: 251-17  December 5, 2017  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   an   entity   known   as   2017   Eagle   Rock   West   Orange ,   LLC   (the   “ Applicant ” )   is  in   the   process   of   acquiring   the   property   at   471   Pleasant   Valley   Way   and   662   Eagle   Rock  Avenue,   also   known   as   Block   176.05,   Lots   12,   12.13   and   14   on   the   Tax   Map   of   the   Township   of  West Orange   currently owned by the Pleasantdale Presbyterian Church   (the   “ Property ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Applicant   is   currently   before   the   West   Orange   Zoning   Board   of  Adjustment   (the   “ Zoning   Board ” )   with   an   application   to   construct   a   3,470   square   foot   Chase  Bank on the Property; and  WHEREAS,   as   part   of   the   Applicant ’ s   application   before   the   Zoning   Board,   the   Applicant  has   expressed   its   desire   to   dedicate   to   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   a n  approximate   19,702   square   foot   completed   and   improved   lot   with   3 6   parking   spaces   (the  “ Dedicated Property ” ) for use by the public; and  WHEREAS,   the   Applicant   has   requested   confirmation   from   the   Township   that   it   will  accept   the   Dedicated   Property   pursuant   to   a   proposed   Deed,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A ”  which   transfer   title   of   the   Dedicated   Property   to   the   Township   while   reserving   certain   easements  through the Dedicated Property;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Applicant   has   performed   an   Environmental   Site   Assessment   (the  “ Assessment ” ) on the Property which includes the Dedicated Property; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Applicant   has   provided   to   the   Township   a   Reliance   Letter,   annexed  hereto as Exhibit   “ B ”   that authorizes the Township to rely upon the Assessment ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   has   conferred   with   its   environmental   consultants   who   have  reviewed the Assessment and the Reliance Letter; and  WHEREAS ,   the   App licant   has   also   advised   that   it   will   provide   for   the   costs   of   a   standard  title policy.

2  NOW   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   accept   the  Dedicated   Property   subject   to   the   Zoning   Board ’ s   approval   of   the   Applicant ’ s   Application   to   the  Zoning Board;   and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Mayor   is   authorized   to   execute   any   and   all   documents   related   to  the   dedication   of   the   Property   and   the   Township ’ s   acceptance   of   title   to   the   Dedicated   Property ;  and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office  in accordance with applicable law.  Karen   J.   Carnevale   Jo e   Krakoviak  Municipal   Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   December 5, 2017  4833-1256-9425, v. 1
Exhibit   “A”

Signatures . The Grantor signs this Deed as of the date at the top of the first page. pages.text: 254-17  December 5, 2017  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange ’ s   Insurance   Broker   solicited   offers   and  proposals for Stop Loss Coverage for the Township ’ s 201 8   Health Insurance   Coverage;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Township ’ s   broker   recommends   the   proposal   submitted   by   Sun   Life  Financial   ( “ Sun Life ” ), annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A; ”   and  WHEREAS,   the   Township ’ s   broker   suggests   that   the   proposal   from   Sun   Life   offers   a  premium that is $50,083 less than the current Stop Loss Coverage ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Mayor   has   recused   himself   from   the   award   of   this   Agreement,   including  the execution of any documents related to this Agreement.  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   shall   and   hereby   does   authorize   the  acceptance   and   award   of   a   contract   with   Sun   Life   for   Stop   Loss   Coverage   for   the   Township ’ s  201 8   Health   Insurance   Coverage ,   consistent   with   the   rates   set   forth   in   the   proposal   annexed  hereto as Exhibit   “ A ” ; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Business   Administrator   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   all  documents necessary to effectuate   the   agreement ; and be it further  RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   Office  for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Joe Krakoviak, Council President  Municipal Clerk  Adopted: December 5, 2017  4846-3014-9975, v. 1
Exhibit   “A”

2532-17  AN ORDINANCE   ESTABLISHING   CHAPTER   23 , SECTION   5   OF THE REVISED  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ( TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE SKATE PARK )  BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   MUNICIPAL   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF  WEST   ORANGE,   NEW   JERSEY   that   Chapter   23   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the  Township of West Orange be and   are   hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The   purpose   of   this   Ordinance   is   to   establish   rules   for   the   Township   of   West   Orange  Skate Park .  II.   CHAPTER   23 ,   SECTION   5 . 1   SHALL   BE   AND   INCORPORATE   THE  FOLLOWING:  23 - 5   TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE SKATE PARK  23 - 5 .1   Purpose .  This   section   establishes   a   set   of   rules   for   the   use   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Skate   Park   for  all persons utilizing the facility.  II I .   CHAPTER   23 ,   SECTION   5 . 2   SHALL   BE   AND   INCORPORATE   THE  FOLLOWING :  23-5.2   Township of West Orange Skate Park Rules and Regulations .  a.   The skate park is a skate at your own risk facility designed for skateboarding only. The  skate park will not be supervised and users will skate at their own risk.  b.   The skate park will open, weather permitting, during posted hours only. Monday through  Saturday 9:00 am to Sunset and Sundays 10:00 am to Sunset  c.   No skating is permitted when the equipment is wet and/or icy.  d.   All participants under the age of 17 must wear a helmet as mandated by New Jersey state  law. Helmets, knee, elbow and wrist pads are strongly recommended for all skaters.  e.   All participants must wear proper footwear and clothing at all times.  f.   Only skaters are allowed in the skate park area. No spectator will be allowed in the  skating area.  g.   All participants must skate in such a manner to avoid collision with other skaters.  h.   The use of profanity or abusive language, vandalism, fighting and other aggressive or  disruptive behavior will result in the loss of privileges at the skate park.  i.   Graffiti or purposeful defacing of the park and/or equipment will result in prosecution.  j.   No food or drink is permitted inside the skate park.  k.   The use of drugs or alcohol within the skate park is prohibited and will result in  prosecution.  l.   Smoking is not permitted in any area of the park.  m.   Pets are not permitted within the skate park.

2  n.   No organized events of any kind are allowed without the approval of the West Orange  Recreation Department.  o.   Skateboarding is discouraged outside the skate park.  p.   All other Township of West Orange Park Rules and Regulations apply.  q.   Failure to abide by these rules and regulations may result in the suspension of an  individual ’ s ability to use the skate park or the issuance of a fine by the Township of  West Orange.  I V .   CHAPTER   23 ,   SECTION   5 . 3   SHALL   BE   AND   INCORPORATE   THE  FOLLOWING :  23 - 5 .3   Violations and Penalties  Any person convicted of violating   any section   this section shall be subject to :  a.   A   f ine   of   not   less   than   thirty-five   ( $ 3 5 )   dollars   and   no   more   than   five   hundred   ($500)  dollars;   and  b.   Suspension of   the person ’ s   ability to the use the Township of West Orange Skate Park.  Any   violation   of   the   provision   of   this   section   which   also   violates   any   other   federal,   state,   or   local  law shall not preclude prosecution under of those federal, state or local laws.  V .   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any   Ordinances   of   the   Township   which   are   in   conflict   with   this   Ordinance   are   hereby  repealed to the extent of such conflict.  VI .   SEVERABILITY  If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   parts   shall   be   severed   and   the  invalidity thereof shall not affect the remaining parts of   this Ordinance.  VI I .   EFFECTIVE DATE  This   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   final   passage   and   publication   in   accordance   with  the law.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Joe Krakoviak, Council President  Municipal Clerk  Robert D. Parisi  Mayor  Introduced: November 21, 2017  Adopted: December 5, 2017
3  2532-17  Legislative History  This   Ordinance   is   intended   to   create   and   adopt   rules   for   the   use   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange Skate Park .  4839-0531-2597, v. 1

2533-17  A N ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING   CHAPTER 7, TRAFFIC, SUBSECTION 7-32.2,  RESTRICTED PARKING ZONES,   OF THE REVISED GEN E RAL ORDI N ANCES  OF   THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BE IT ORDAINED,   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, in the County of  Essex, State of New Jersey as follows:  Section 1.   Subsection 7-32.2, Restricted Parking Zones, of the Revised General Ordinances  of the Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows ( italic type denotes  deletion,   bold type denotes addition):  7-32.2   Restricted Parking Zones. Pursuant to N.J.S.A 39:4-197.6, Restricted Parking Zones, as  defined in N.J.S.A. 39:4-207.6e, are established in front of the following residences as indicated on the  Tax Maps of the Township of West Orange, at the specified locations. Parking within a specific zone is  restricted to the motor vehicle issued a permit pursuant to N.J.S.A. 39:4-197.7. In accordance with  N.J.S.A., 39:4-197.6 parking is permitted in the Restricted Parking Zone except when prohibited in front  of the residence by other sections of this Chapter.  Residence Address   No. of Spaces   Location  82 Valley Way   1   Beginning 25 feet north of the  northerly curbline of Maple Street  extended and extending 18 feet  north.  24 Park Terrace   1   Beginning 373 feet west of the  westerly curbline of Main Street  extended and extending 22 feet  west.  Section 2.   If any provision of this Ordinance or application thereof, under any  circumstances, is held invalid, the invalidity shall not affect any other provisions or applications of this

Ordinance that can be given effect without the invalid provision(s) or application(s) and to this end the  provisions of this Ordinance are severable.  Section 3.   All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this Ordinance are  hereby repealed.  Section 4.   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication in  accordance with law and upon the approval of the New Jersey Department of Transportation if required.  ________________________________________  ROBERT D. PARISI, MAYOR  _________________________________________  JOE KRAKOVIAK, COUNCIL PRESIDENT  ___________________________________  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, R.M.C., MUNICIPAL CLERK  Introduced: November 21, 2017  Adopted: December 5, 2017

2533-17  L EGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance is in response to requests from handicapped residents who reside at 82 Valley  Way and 24 Park Terrace. By adopting this Ordinance, the Township will establish a handicapped  parking stall adjacent to each of these addresses. Such parking stalls are permissible under the laws of  New Jersey, and they are designated as Restricted Parking Zones.

Pleasant Valley Way   Both   40 MPH   From   the center of   Northfield  Avenue to the center of State  Highway No. 10, Mt. Pleasant  Avenue, except for a school  Speed limit of 25 MPH from  490   f eet north of the center of  Brook End Drive to   5 8 5   feet  n orth of the center of  Underwood Drive .  Section 2.   Subsection 7-33, School Zones, of the Revised General Ordinance of the  Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows (Italic Type denotes  deletion,   bold type denotes addition):

School   Street   Location  Golda Och Academy,   Pleasant Valley Way   From   4 9 0   feet north of  Eric F. Ross Upper School Campus   the center of Brook End Drive  to   585   feet north of the center  of Underwood Drive.  Section 3 .   If any provision s   of this Ordinance or application thereof, under any  circumstances,   is held invalid, the invalidity shall not   affect   any other provisions or applications of this  Ordinance that can be given effect without the invalid provision(s) or application(s) and to this end the  provisions of this Ordinance are severable.  Section   4 .   All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this  Ordinance are hereby repealed.  Section   5 .   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication in  accordance with law   and upon the approval by Resolution of the Essex County Board of Chosen  Freeholders .  ________________________________________  ROBERT D. PARISI, MAYOR  _________________________________________  JOE KRAKOVIAK, COUNCIL PRESIDENT  ___________________________________  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, R.M.C., MUNICIPAL CLERK  Introduced: November 21, 2017  Adopted: December 5, 2017

2534-17  L EGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance amends the   speed   lim it along Pleasant Valley Way between Northfield Avenue  and State Highway No. 10, Mt. Pleasant Avenue, to 40 miles per hour. The current speed limit is 50  mile s   per hour from Northfield Avenue to Cornell Street and 45 miles per hour from Cornell Street to  State Highway No. 10. It also establishes a   S chool   Z one in the vicinity of the Golda Och Academy   located  at 1418 Pleasant Valley Way   and a School Zone speed limit of 25 miles per hour at the school. The speed  limit reduction and school zone speed limit w ere   recommended in a study dated September 20, 2017  prepared by NV5, 7 Campus Drive, Suite 300, Parsippany, N.J. 07054 for the Essex County Division of  Engineering.

pages.text: 222-17  October 24, 2017  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Police   Department   (the   “ WOPD ” )   seeks   to  utilize   the   New   Jersey   State   Police   Training   Facility   in   Totowa,   New   Jersey   (the   “ Facility ” )   for  Active   Shooter   Training   exercises   (the   “ Exercises ” )   for   officers   hired   after   the   February   2016  Exercises held within the Township of West Orange; and  WHEREAS,   the   State   of   New   Jersey   requires   the   execution   of   a   Release   and   Hold  Harmless   Agreement   (the   “ Release ” ),   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A, ”   for   use   of   the   Facility   for  the Exercises; and  WHEREAS,   the   State   of   New   Jersey   allowing   the   WOPD   to   utilize   the   Facility   for   the  Exercises at no cost to the Township of West Orange.  NOW   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   execute   the  Release annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A; ”   and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Police   Chief   for   the   Township   of   West   Orange   i s   authorized   to  execute   the Release on behalf of the Township; and   be it further  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office  in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J.   Carnevale , R.M.C.   Joe   Krakoviak  Municipal   Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   October 24, 2017  4841-8857-2495, v. 1

E xhibit   “ A ”  RELEASE AND HOLD HARMLESS AGREEMENT  In   consideration   for   license   to   enter   upon   and   use   the   property,   including   buildings,  facilities,   pool   and   grounds   owned   by   State   of   New   Jersey   (the   “ Premises ” )   for   Active   Shooter  training   activities   (the   “ Training ” )   conducted   by   West   Orange   Police   Department   (the   “ WOPD ” ),  the Township of West Orange (the   “ Township ” ):  1.   Hereby   releases,   waives,   discharges   and   covenants   not   to   sue   State   of   New   Jersey   for   any   and  all   damage   and   any   claim   or   demands   therefore   arising   from   the   use   of   the   Premises   by  members   of   the   WOPD   for   the   Training   arising   from   or   out   of   the   negligence   of   WOPD,   its  representatives,   agents   and   employees,   related   to   the   use   of   the   Premises   by   WOPD   for   the  Training, subject to the provisions of the New Jersey Tort Claims Act   (N.J.S.A.   59: 1-1 et seq.  and the New Jersey Contractual Liability Act (N.J.S.A. 59:13-1 et seq.).  2.   Hereby   agrees   to   hold   harmless   the   State   of   New   Jersey   from   and   against   any   and   all   claims,  actions,   damages,   liability   and   expense   in   connection   with   loss   of   life,   personal   injury,   damage  to   property   or   any   other   claim   asserted   whatsoever,   arising   from   or   out   of   the   negligence   of  WOPD,   its   representatives,   agents   and   employees,   related   to   the   use   of   the   Premises   by   WOPD  for   the   Training,   subject   to   the   provisions   of   the   New   Jersey   Tort   Claims   Act   (N.J.S.A.   59:   1-1  et seq. and the New Jersey Contractual Liability Act (N.J.S.A. 59:13-1 et seq.).  3.   Hereby   assumes   full   and   sole   responsibility   for   compliance   with   any   and   all   local,   state   or  federal   laws,   ordinances,   regulations   or   rules   applicable   to   the   use   of   the   Premises   for   the  purposes described herein.  The   undersigned   represents   and   warrants   that   he   or   she   has   full   authority   to   enter   into   this  agreement   has   entered   into   this   agreement   voluntarily   after   reading   and   understanding   it,   and   that  no   other   oral   representations,   statements   or   inducements   apart   from   the   foregoing   written   agreement  have been made.  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  WEST ORANGE POLICE DEPARTMENT  By:  Date:  4818-2992-2639, v. 1

702193  224-17  October 24, 2017  RESOLUTION   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   IN   THE  COUNTY   OF   ESSEX ,   NEW   JERSEY   AUTHORIZING   AN   EMERGENCY  APPROPRIATION   PURSUANT   TO   N.J.S.A.   40A:4-48   TO   FUND  AMOUNTS   OWING   TO   OTHERS   FOR   TAXES   LEVIED   AND   FURTHER  AUTHORIZING   THE   ISSUANCE   OF   EMERGENCY   NOTES   TO   FUND  SUCH EMERGENCY APPROPRIATION  WHEREAS,   an   emergency   has   arisen   requiring   the   appropriation   of   funds   of   the  Township   of   West   Orange ,   in   the   County   of   Essex ,   New   Jersey   (the   “ Township ” )   in   the  amount   of   $ 3 , 200 ,000   to   provide   for   amounts   owed   by   the   Township   to   the   owners   of  various   properties   for   taxes   levied   in   the   Township   (as   more   particularly   described   on  the   List   of   Settled   Appeals   available   for   inspection   in   the   office   of   the   Township   Clerk)  (the   “ Tax Appeal   Settlement s ” ) ;   and  WHEREAS,   the   total   amount   of   emergency   appropriations   created,   including   the  appropriation   to   be   created   by   this   resolutio n,   is   $ 3 , 200 ,000 ,   and   3%   of   the   total   current  and utility   operating appropriations in the budget for   C Y 20 1 7   is   $ 2,400,197.91 ; and  WHEREAS,   the   foregoing   emergency   appropriation,   together   with   prior  emergency   appropriations,   exceed s   3%   of   the   total   operating   appropriations   in   the  budget for   CY   20 1 7 ; and  W HEREAS ,   it   is   therefore   necessary   to   request   the   Director   of   the   Division   of  L o cal   Government   Services   (the   “ Director ” )   to   determine   that   the   emergency   exists   and  to   fix   the   amount   of   the   emergency   approp r iation   in   an   amount   not   exceeding  $3,200,000   and   to   seek   the   approval   of   the   Local   Finance   Board   for   a   refunding   bond  ordinance in an amount not to exceed $3,200,000 to fund the emergency appropriation;  NOW ,   THEREFORE ,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   (by   not   less   than   two-thirds   of   the  full membership of the governing body affirmatively concurring)   AS FOLLOWS :  1.   Pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:4-4 9 ,   a n   emergency   appropriation   is   hereby  made   in   the   amount   of   $ 3 , 200 ,000   for   court   approved   Tax   Appeal  S ettlem e nt s .   The   $ 3 , 200 ,000   appropriated   herein   is   sufficient   to   fund   this  purpose .  2.   The   Township   intends   that   t he   emergency   appropriation   set   forth   in  Section   1   hereof   shall   be   provided   for   through   the   adoption   of   a   refunding  bond   ordinance   entitled,   “ Refunding   Bond   Ordinance   Provid ing   Funding  for   an   Emergency   Approp r iation   for   Payment   of   Amounts   Owing   to   Others  for   Taxes   Levied   in   and   by   the   Township   of   West   Orange ,   in   the   County   of  Essex,   New   Jersey,   Appropriating   $ 3 , 200 ,000   Therefor   and   Authorizing  the   Issuance   of   $ 3 , 200 ,000   Bonds   or   Notes   of   the   Township   for   Financing

702193  the   Cost   Thereof , ”   which   bond   ordinance   is   anticipated   to   be   introduced  and finally adopted prior to December 31, 201 7 .  3.   An   emergency   note   not   in   excess   of   $3, 2 00,000   is   hereby   authorized   to   be  issued   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:4-51   in   the   event   funding   is   not   provided  as set forth in section   2 .  4.   The   emergency   note   authorized   herein,   if   issued,   shall   be   executed   by   the  Mayor   and   the   Chief   Financial   Officer   of   the   Township   and   attested   by   the  Township   Clerk.  5.   The   Chief   Financial   Officer   of   the   Township   is   hereby   delegated   the  authority   to   sell   and   award   the   note,   if   any,   and   such   note,   if   issued,   may  be   renewed   from   time   to   time   by   the   Chief   Financial   Officer   provided   that  any such renewals shall be payable on or   before December 31,   2018 .  6.   In   accordance   with   N.J.S.A.   40A:4-49,   petition   shall   be   made   to   the  Director   for   permission   to   exceed   the   statutory   limitation   of   3   percent   for  the   creation   of   one   or   more   appropriations   for   the   purposes   set   forth   in   the  preamble in accordance with this   r esolution.  7.   This   emergency   resolution   super s edes   and   amends   the   resolution   no.   28-  17   adopted   January   3,   2017 ,   as   amended   by   resolution   no.   50-17   adopted  February 7, 2017 ,   previously providing $2,000,0 0 0 for tax appeals.  8.   Two   certified   copies   of   this   resolution   shall   be   filed   with   the   Director   along  with   the   request   for   approval   of   this   emerg en cy   and   the   emergency  approp r iations   in   the   total   amount   of   $3, 2 00,000   for   the   purpose   described  herein .  9.   This resolution shall take effect immediately.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Joe Krakoviak, Council President  Municipal Clerk  Adopted: October 24, 2017

pages.text: 225-17  October 25, 2017  RESOLUTION   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   IN   THE  COUNTY   OF   ESSEX ,   NEW   JERSEY   MAKING   APPLICATION   TO   THE  LOCAL FINANCE BOARD PURSUANT TO N.J.S.A. 40A:2-51  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange ,   in   the   County   of   Essex ,   New  Jersey   (the   “ Township ” )   desires   to   make   application   to   the   Local   Finance   Board  pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:2-51   for   its   review   and   approval   of   a   refunding   bond   ordinance  to fund an emergency appropriation for amounts owed to others for taxes levied; and  WHEREAS , the Township believes that:  (a)   it is in the public interest to accomplish this purpose;  (b)   the   purpose   is   for   the   health,   the   welfare,   the   convenience   or   the  betterment of the inhabitants of the Township;  (c)   the   amounts   to   be   expended   for   the   purpose   are   not   unreasonable   or  exorbitant; and  (d)   the   purpose   is   an   efficient   and   feasible   means   of   providing   services  for   the   needs   of   the   inhabitants   of   the   Township   and   will   not   create   an  undue financial burden to be placed upon the Township;  NOW ,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL  OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   IN   THE   COUNTY   OF   ESSEX ,   NEW  JERSEY AS FOLLOWS:  Section 1 .   The   application   to   the   Local   Finance   Board   is   hereby  approved,   and   the   Township ’ s   Bond   Counsel   and   Financial   Advisor ,   along   with   other  representatives   of   the   Township,   are   hereby   authorized   to   prepare   such   application   and  to represent the Township in matters pertaining thereto.  Section 2 .   The   Clerk   of   the   Township   is   hereby   directed   to   prepare   and  to   file   copies   of   the   proposed   bond   ordinance   with   the   Local   Finance   Board   as   part   of  such application.  Section 3 .   The   Local   Finance   Board   is   hereby   respectfully   requested   to  consider   such   application   and   to   record   its   findings,   recommendations   and   approvals   as  provided by the applicable New Jersey Statute.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Joe Krakoviak, Council President  Municipal Clerk  Adopted: October 24, 2017

Recorded Vote  AYE   NO   ABSTAIN   ABSENT  The   foregoing   is   a   true   copy   of   a   resolution   adopted   by   the   governing   body   of   the  Township of West Orange   on   October __, 2017 .  Karen J. C arn e vale,   R.M.C.  Municipal   Clerk

pages.text: 226-17  October 24, 2017  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS , the State of New Jersey Board of Public Utilities awarded to Willdan Energy, 200  Middlesex Essex Turnpike, Suite 304, Iselin, NJ 08830 a contract for the New Jersey Clean Energy Direct  Install Program on August 24, 2016; and  WHEREAS , the Township of West Orange is participating in the NJ Clean Energy Direct Install  Program for 10 facilities in the Township of which a summary of the costs, the projected savings, and the  estimated payback period of the investment is included; and  WHEREAS , the total cost of improvements totals $218,484.24 of which the Township ’ s contributory  portion totals $65,545.27; and  WHEREAS , the BPU Energy Assessment calculations show an annual savings at $76,281.12 with a  payback period of approximately 0.86 years on the Township funded portion; and  WHEREAS , it is the recommendation of the Township Administrator to award this contract to: Willdan  Energy 200 Middlesex Essex Turnpike, Suite 304, Iselin, NJ 08830; and  WHEREAS , the Chief Financial Officer has certified that funds are available in the budget; the line item  appropriation or ordinance to be charged is line account identified below; and  NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED , by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange,  County of Essex, State of New Jersey, that the contract be awarded to Willdan Energy, 200 Middlesex  Essex Turnpike, Suite 304, Iselin, NJ 08830 in the amount of $65,545.27.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange that the  Mayor   is hereby authorized to execute the agreements and other related documents on   behalf of the  Township of West Orange. Williams  be and they are hereby appointed as the Township of West Orange Community Development  representatives to the Essex County Division of Housing and Community  Development for the year   20 18 .
