-2-  INTERIM REDEVELOPMENT AGREEMENT  THIS AGREEMENT,   entered into this _____ day of March 2017 (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement") between the   TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE , a public body corporate (which, together with any successor public body or officer hereinafter designated by or pursuant to law, is hereinafter referred to as the "Township"), having its offices at 66 Main Street, West Orange, New Jersey 07052, and   THE ALPERT GROUP, LLC   with offices located at 1 Parker Plaza, Fort Lee, New Jersey 07024 (hereinafter referred to as “Alpert” or the "Interim Redeveloper").  WITNESSETH:  WHEREAS,   pursuant to the provisions of the Local Redevelopment and Housing Law, as amended and supplemented, N.J.S.A. 40A:12A –1 et seq. (the “Local Redevelopment and Housing Law”), the Township is responsible for implementing redevelopment plans and carrying out redevelopment projects in the Township; and  WHEREAS , the Interim Redeveloper is in the business of rehabilitating, adaptively reusing and redeveloping properties and improvements; and  WHEREAS , in accordance with the criteria set forth in the Local Redevelopment and Housing Law, the Township passed Resolution No. 228-16 which designated as “an area in need of redevelopment” a series of parcels of property referred to as the Central Valley Redevelopment Area   (hereinafter   referred   to   collectively   as   the   “Redevelopment   Area”)   and   adopted   a redevelopment plan for the area as may be further amended and supplemented from time to time, (hereinafter referred to as the “Redevelopment Plan”); and  WHEREAS ,   the   Redevelopment   Area   designated   by   the   Township   consists   of approximately 2.5 acres and specifically includes parcels of property identified as Block 9, Lots 11.01, 11.02, 11.03, 32, & 36 and Block 7, Lot 22 as each respective parcel of property is designated upon the official Tax Maps of the Township of West Orange (the “Parcels”); and  WHEREAS , the Parcels are included as part of the Central Valley Brownfields Development Area (the “BDA”); and

-3-  WHEREAS , the Parcels are presently owned by various parties, including the Township which has obtained a final judgment in an In Rem Tax Foreclosure pertaining to parcels identified as Block 9, Lot 36 and Block 7, Lot 22; and  WHEREAS , following the final judgment in the In Rem Tax Foreclosure, the Township has obtained title to a vacant lot known as Block 7, Lot 22 (the “Selecto-Flash Vacant Lot”), and Block 9, Lot 36 (the “Selecto-Flash Building Property”); and  WHEREAS,   a copy of the Redevelopment Plan, as constituted on the effective date of this Agreement, has been filed in the Office of the Clerk of the Township, located at 66 Main Street, West Orange, New Jersey 07052; and  WHEREAS,   the Local Redevelopment and Housing Law authorizes the Township to arrange or contract with a redeveloper for the planning, construction or undertaking of any project or redevelopment work in an area designated as an area in need of redevelopment; and  WHEREAS , the Interim Redeveloper has engaged in various negotiations with the Township regarding its proposal to be designated redeveloper until December 31, 2019 (the “Term”) during which 1) the Interim Redeveloper shall demolish the current buildings located on the Selecto-Flash Building Property as an initial step towards implementation of the Redevelopment Plan; 2) the Township will undertake remediation of environmental contamination of the Selecto-Flash Building Property; provided funding is available; and 3) based on the outcome of the remediation effort, the Interim Redeveloper may exercise the option to file an application for land use approvals to develop the Selecto-Flash Building Property consistent with the Redevelopment Plan and to purchase that Property from the Township; and  WHEREAS , the Township has determined that it is in the Township’s best interests to select the Interim Redeveloper to serve as the designated redeveloper for the Redevelopment Area for the Term to implement steps toward the Redevelopment Plan as set forth herein; and  WHEREAS , during the Term, the Interim Redeveloper will have the exclusive right to file a land use application with the Township with regards to the Selecto-Flash Building Property with no

-4- promises or commitment as to its acceptance; but, if such an application is approved, with the further right to acquire the Selecto-Flash Building Property under terms set forth herein; and  WHEREAS,   the Township and the Interim Redeveloper have engaged in such negotiations and the Township has determined that in furtherance of the Township’s objectives to implement the redevelopment contemplated in the Redevelopment Plan, it is in the Township’s best interests to enter into this Agreement with the Interim Redeveloper; and  WHEREAS,   Interim Redeveloper acknowledges that all uses to which the Redevelopment Area may be devoted are controlled by the Redevelopment Plan and this Agreement, and that under no circumstances shall the Interim Redeveloper undertake any demolition, rehabilitation, or construction on or development of the Redevelopment Area unless it is materially in accordance with   the   Redevelopment   Plan   and   this   Agreement,   and   that   Interim   Redeveloper   further understands and acknowledges that if any part of the Redevelopment Area is developed and used in a manner inconsistent with the Agreement or the Redevelopment Plan, the Township shall have the right as more specifically set forth herein to terminate this Agreement; and  WHEREAS,   the Township and the Interim Redeveloper desire to enter into this Agreement for the purpose of setting forth in greater detail their respective undertakings, rights and obligations in connection with the Redevelopment, all in accordance with the Redevelopment Plan, applicable law and the terms and conditions of this Agreement hereinafter set forth.  NOW, THEREFORE,   for and in consideration of the mutual promises, covenants and agreements contained herein, and other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged by each of the parties, and for the benefit of the parties   hereto   and   general   public,   and,   further   to   implement   the   purposes   of   the   Local Redevelopment and Housing Law and the Redevelopment Plan, the parties hereto agree as follows:

-5-  DEFINITIONS  Defined Terms.   The parties hereto agree that, unless the context otherwise specifies or requires, the following terms shall have the meanings specified below, such definitions to be applicable equally to the singular and plural forms of such terms and to the use of the upper or lower case initial letter of each word contained in such terms.  Agreement :   The scope of this Redevelopment Agreement includes certain activities identified herein to further the redevelopment of the Selecto-Flash Building Property within the Township of West Orange, County of Essex and State of New Jersey.  Applicable Law : Any and all federal, state and local laws, rules, regulations, statutes and ordinances applicable to the Project, or to any aspect of this Agreement.  Concrete Testing:   On February 21, 2017, the Township approved a proposal by Matrix New World to test the Selecto-Flash Building Property’s concrete. It is expected that those results will   be   available   by   March   21,   2017   (“Concrete   Test”).   If   the   Concrete   Testing   reveals concentrations of contaminants in excess of permissible levels for re-use of the concrete on-site, then the Township shall either (i) remove that concrete, at its cost and expense, after it is crushed and stockpiled by the Interim Redeveloper; or (ii) either party may terminate this Agreement on twenty (20) days notice.  Demolition Timetable :   That schedule set forth in Paragraph 2.06 which designates the Interim   Redeveloper’s   deadlines   in   completing   the   Demolition   Project   consistent   with   this Agreement.  Days :   Whenever the word "days" is used to denote time, it shall mean calendar days, except where business days are specifically mentioned in the text.   Business days are Monday through Friday and do not include Saturdays, Sundays, and Holidays.  Default:   Any condition, event, act or omission on the part of or in connection with either party’s performance or lack thereof of the terms and obligations of this Agreement giving rise to an Event of Default as defined in Article V herein.

-6-  Demolition Project : Shall mean the demolition and crushing of the structures located on the Selecto-Flash Building Property and rough grading of the Selecto-Flash Building Property in accordance with the Proposal annexed hereto as Exhibit   “A . ”   The scope of this Demolition Project does not include the removal of crushed concrete from the Selecto Flash Building Property nor the removal of any tanks or other unforeseen underground structures discovered at any time.   This definition shall include all reasonably necessary measures associated with the demolition of the building located on the Selecto-Flash Building Property including all appropriate applications and notices to appropriate government bodies and/or agencies and/or utilities and the obtaining of all required permits, except that testing of concrete shall be conducted by the Township within thirty (30) days of the execution of this Agreement.  Effective Date :   The date this Agreement is approved by the Township Council and executed by the Mayor.  EPA (or USEPA):   The United States Environmental Protection Agency.  Events of Default : Defined in Paragraph 5.01 herein.  Financial Institution :   A bank, savings bank, savings and loan association, mortgage lender or insurance company, pension fund, real estate investment trust, investment bank, Economic and/or Redevelopment Authority or similarly recognized reputable source of construction or permanent financing for the Project which may be chartered under the laws of the United States of America, or any State thereof.  Force Majeure :   As used in Paragraph 8.01 herein, shall mean acts of God, fire, earthquake, explosion, the elements, war, riots, mob violence or civil disturbance, inability to procure or a general shortage of labor, equipment or facilities, energy, materials or supplies in the open market, failure of transportation, strikes, walkouts, actions of labor unions, court orders, laws, rules, regulations or orders of governmental or public agencies, bodies and authorities, or any other similar cause not within the control of the Interim Redeveloper and any delays in the receipt of any necessary Governmental Approvals despite the diligent efforts of Interim Redeveloper to obtain

-7- same.  Governmental Applications:   Shall mean any and all submissions, supporting documents, reports or other proofs transmitted to any State, federal or local governmental office, Township authority, department, officer or agent for the purpose of obtaining authorization or approval of any aspect of the Project.  Governmental Approvals : Any approvals, authorizations, permits, licenses and certificates needed from governmental authorities having jurisdiction, whether federal, state, county or local, to the extent necessary to implement the Project or any aspect thereof in accordance with the Applicable Law, the Redevelopment Plan and this Agreement.  Insurance Requirements :   All requirements set forth in the terms of any insurance policy(ies) covering or applicable to all or any part of the Project, the Selecto Flash Building Property or applicable to any Improvements thereon, or with respect to any portion of the Selecto Flash Building Property, or any easement, license, or other right of access for the benefit of the Interim Redeveloper granted by the Township, all requirements of the issuer of any such policy, and all orders, rules, regulations and other requirements of the National Board of Fire Underwriters (or any other body exercising similar functions) applicable to or affecting all or any portion of the Project, the Selecto Flash Building Property, the Improvements thereon or the use or condition thereof. The Interim Redeveloper and any contractors or sub-contractors engaged by the Interim Redeveloper shall provide a Certificate of Insurance naming the Township and Interim Redeveloper as additional insured with coverage in the amounts set forth in Paragraph 2.10 herein.  Interim Redeveloper : The Alpert Group, LLC or its designee; provided, however, that the Alpert Group, LLC shall remain fully liable for all obligations hereunder. Any assignment must be approved by approved by the Township after submission of all required financial information which approval shall not be unreasonably withheld.  Local Redevelopment and Housing Law : N.J.S.A. 40A:12A-1, et seq., and as same may be amended from time to time.

-8-  NJDEP (or DEP) : The State of New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection.  Notice to Proceed:   A notice issued by the Township to the Interim Redeveloper to proceed with the Demolition Project, to be issued after completion of pre-demolition concrete testing;  Planning Board : The Township of West Orange Planning Board and any successor thereto exercising similar functions in accordance with the Municipal Land Use Law, N.J.S.A. 40:55D-1 et seq.  Project :   The Demolition Project as defined herein, as well as any redevelopment of the Redevelopment Area consistent with the Redevelopment Plan.  Redevelopment Area (or the Parcels) : Those parcels of property identified as Block 9, Lots 11.01, 11.02, 11.03, 32, & 36 and Block 7, Lot 22 upon the official Tax Maps of the Township of West Orange of property designated by the governing body of the Township of West Orange as the Central Avenue Redevelopment Area pursuant to the Local Redevelopment and Housing Law and as may be amended.  Redevelopment Plan :   The Central Avenue Redevelopment Plan, as adopted and amended by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, and as it may hereafter be amended from time to time in accordance with the law.  Redevelopment Proposal :   The formal proposal submitted by the Interim Redeveloper setting forth a specific design and plan for all or any portion of the Redevelopment Area.   The Interim Redeveloper shall have the exclusive right to submit a Redevelopment Proposal to the Township during the Term of this Agreement. The Township agrees to review that Redevelopment Proposal consistent with the provisions of the LRHL and the Municipal Land Use Law.  Selecto Flash Building Property : Property identified as Block 9, Lot 36 on the Tax Map of the Township of West Orange.  Term:   The duration of this Agreement shall be from the Effective Date until December 31, 2019, subject to extensions under the terms set forth herein.  Township : The party defined as such in the Recitals of this Agreement, together with any

-9- successor(s) thereto, and is specifically defined here as the Township of West Orange, its successors or assigns.  ARTICLE I TITLE TO THE PARCELS WITHIN THE REDEVELOPMENT AREA 1.01.   Current Ownership to Maintain Title.   The designation of the Interim Redeveloper shall   not   affect any ownership or title to any of the parcels within the Redevelopment Area , unless the Parties agree that the Township shall convey any parcel(s) to the Interim Redeveloper pursuant to the terms of this Agreement  1.02.   Notice of Current Licensee.   The Township has entered into a license agreement with Richard Behar d/b/a Roseland Landscaping (the “Current Licensee”) for a portion of the Selecto Flash Building Property whereby the Current Licensee is authorized to store equipment and vehicles. A copy of the License Agreement is annexed to this Agreement as Exhibit “ C ”.  ARTICLE II INTERIM REDEVELOPER’S RESPONSIBILITIES  2.01.   Interim Redeveloper Designation . The Alpert Group, LLC is hereby designated the Interim Redeveloper for   the Selecto-Flash Building Property subject to the terms and conditions herein. 2.02   Demolition Project.   The Interim Redeveloper shall demolish the structures located on the Selecto-Flash Building Property, crush the debris, and rough grade the Selecto-Flash Building Property as per the attached Proposal and in accordance with the schedule set forth in Paragraph 2.06 herein.  2.03.   Demolition Costs . The Interim Redeveloper shall be responsible for all costs set forth in the Proposal attached hereto as Exhibit   “A,”   subject to Paragraph 3.05 of this Agreement.  2.04.   Monthly Reports to the Township of Demolition Costs.   The Interim Redeveloper shall maintain a record of all direct costs related to the Demolition including identification of all contractors, copies of cancelled checks and other supporting documentation to support all

-10- disbursements made in connection with the Demolition Project (the “Demolition Costs”) and shall provide a monthly report to the Township showing the Demolition Costs incurred during that month. The first report shall be due no later than ten (10) days after the first full month following the Effective Date of this Agreement (the “Monthly Report”). Each subsequent Monthly Report shall be due one month thereafter from the previous report due date.   All monthly reports shall be transmitted via electronic mail to the following: Name   Title   Email Address Mayor Robert D. Parisi   Mayor   Mayor@westorange.org Karen Carnevale   Municipal Clerk   Kcarnevale@westorange.org Richard D. Trenk, Esq.   Municipal Attorney   Rtrenk@trenklawfirm.com Leonard Lepore   Director of Engineering   LLepore@westorange.org Tom Tracey   Construction Official   TTracey@westorange.org Geneice Gary Adams   Zoning Officer   ZoningOfficial@westorange.org John K. Sayers   Business Administrator   JSayers@westorange.org John Gross   Chief Financial Officer   JGross@westorange.org The Township may designate additional persons to receive the Monthly Report from time to time. The Interim Redeveloper understands that all reports shall be available to the public.  2.05.   Reimbursement of Demolitions Costs .   In the event that this Agreement is terminated by either Party, the Interim Redeveloper shall be entitled to reimbursement of all Demolition Costs from any subsequently designated redeveloper, no later than as a condition of transfer of title from the Township to that subsequently designated redeveloper, or any subsequent owner of the Selecto-Flash Building Property. The Township will designate the Interim Redeveloper as a third party beneficiary in any agreement with any subsequently designated redeveloper with respect to this reimbursement obligation. No interest or other fees shall be added.

-11-  2.06.   Timeline for Demolition . Interim Redeveloper shall proceed with the Demolition consistent with the following timeframe: i.   All required applications for shut off shall be submitted to all appropriate utility company/entities within twenty (20) days of Interim Redeveloper’s receipt of the Notice to Proceed with copies to the Township. ii.   All required demolition permits shall be submitted to the Township’s Construction Department within ten (10) days of receipt of all disconnect notices with copies to the Township. iii.   Physical demolition of the building on the Selecto-Flash Building Property shall commence no later than twenty (20) days after the Township issues the demolition permit. Demolition shall continue day to day until completed, subject to conditions qualifying as force majeure as set forth in this Agreement. iv.   The Selecto-Flash Building Property will be rough graded within ninety (90) days of receipt of all permits authorizing that grading and filling activity. The Township and Interim Redeveloper agree that these timeframes may be extended by mutual written consent by both Parties.  2.07.   Environmental   Remediation .   The   Township   and   Interim   Redeveloper acknowledge the existence of environmental contamination on the Selecto-Flash Building Property and that the Redevelopment Area has been included in the BDA.   The Township and Interim Redeveloper agree that the Interim Redeveloper shall have no liability for the existing environmental conditions on the Selecto-Flash Building Property and no obligation regarding the remediation of the existing environmental conditions on the Redevelopment Area. The Township agrees that it will apply for and diligently pursue funding for the remediation of the Selecto Flash Building Property under the Hazardous Discharge Site Remediation Program or other sources of funding available from the NJDEP.   Assuming the availability of funding, the Parties estimate that the remedial investigation of the Selecto-Flash Building Property will proceed such that a Remedial Action Workplan can be completed in two years from the Effective Date of this Agreement. Within sixty

-12- (60) days of the completion and submission of the RAWP to NJDEP, Interim Redeveloper may choose to terminate this Agreement, at which point neither Party shall retain any liabilities or responsibilities to the other except for the indemnification obligations set forth in Paragraph 2.11 herein and the Interim Redeveloper’s right to reimbursement of Demolition Costs pursuant to Paragraphs 2.05 and 3.05 herein.  2.08.   Access to the Selecto-Flash Building Property .   The Interim Redeveloper is hereby granted a license during the Term of this Agreement to enter onto and into the Selecto- Flash Building Property at its own risk. Simultaneously, the Interim Redeveloper agrees to allow access to the Selecto-Flash Building Property to the Township and its professionals, including but not limited to, the Township’s designated environmental consultants and any and all governmental agencies and entities with jurisdiction related to activities to be performed on to the Selecto-Flash Building Property including, but not limited to, the NJDEP and the EPA, consistent with the Consent for Access to Properties annexed to this Agreement as Exhibit   “B.”   The Interim Redeveloper agrees that it shall not interfere or hinder in any way the ability of the DEP, the EPA, the Township, and/or the Township’s designee to perform environmental testing, sampling, or any other environmental or other site work on the Selecto-Flash Building Property.   During the Term, the Interim Redeveloper shall not have the right to rent or sublet or earn income from the Selecto-Flash Building Property and all income earned or generated shall be the sole property of the Township. .  2.09.   Redevelopment Proposal .   During the Term of this Agreement, the Interim Redeveloper shall have the sole right to submit to the Township a Redevelopment Proposal which shall include a specific design and plan for the use of either the Selecto-Flash Building Property and/or the Redevelopment Area.   The Interim Redeveloper understands, acknowledges, and agrees that the submission of a Redevelopment Proposal is subject to all review and approvals as required under the Local Redevelopment and Housing Law and all other governing regulations, ordinances and law. The Interim Redeveloper also understands, acknowledges, and agrees that any Redevelopment Proposal must comply with the Redevelopment Plan as adopted by the

-13- Township Council. No promises or representations have been made or relied upon in entering into this Agreement. During the Term of this Agreement, the Interim Redeveloper may file applications with the Township under the Municipal Land Use Law, seeking approvals for a Redevelopment Proposal expected to consist of approximately one hundred (100) residential units and possible commercial space, and to State and County agencies where necessary, including but not limited to a Flood Hazard Area Verification and Individual Permit from NJDEP.   Interim Redeveloper will diligently pursue any such applications, with the expectation that final approvals will be granted within one year of the completion and submission of applications. At any time after receipt of approvals of the Redevelopment Proposal pursuant to the Municipal Land Use Law and applicable State and County approvals, Interim Redeveloper may apply for a Long Term Tax Exemption (“LTTE”) pursuant to NJSA 40A:20-1 et seq.   Interim Redeveloper anticipates submitting an LTTE application providing for a Payment In Lieu Of Taxes (“PILOT”) based on 10% of gross revenue if a residential or commercial use is developed on the Property. If the ultimate end use of the Property is a surface parking lot, Interim Redeveloper shall not seek an LTTE. Interim Redeveloper acknowledges that, by virtue of entering into this Agreement, the Township is not obligated to grant an LTTE.   However, should an LTTE not be granted, or the necessary Municipal Land Use Law approvals or other State or County approvals be denied, Interim Redeveloper reserves the right to terminate this Agreement at which point neither Party shall retain any liabilities or responsibilities to the other except for the indemnification obligations set forth in Paragraph 2.11 herein and the Interim Redeveloper’s right to reimbursement of Demolition Costs pursuant to Paragraphs 2.05 and 3.05 herein. At any time after receipt of approvals of the Redevelopment Proposal pursuant to the Municipal Land Use Law and applicable State and County approvals, Interim Redeveloper may exercise the right to purchase the Selecto-Flash Building Property from the Township. Such closing

-14- will take place within sixty (60) days of receipt of final Municipal Land Use Law approvals, barring circumstances constituting force majeure. In the event that Interim Redeveloper exercises its right to purchase the Selecto-Flash Building Property, the purchase price of the Property shall be one million five hundred thousand dollars ($1,500,000) (the “Purchase Price"). No adjustments, credits or offsets shall be available with regard to any of the Interim Redeveloper’s costs and expenses including, but not limited to, the demolition costs.  2.10.   Insurance .   At all times during the Agreement, the Interim Redeveloper shall maintain or cause to be maintained at its own cost and expense, with responsible insurers, the following insurance, with such variations as shall reasonably be required to conform to customary insurance practice: (a)   Builder’s Risk Insurance for the benefit of Interim Redeveloper and the Township, as their interests may appear, during the term of demolition and construction which will protect against loss or damage resulting from fire and lightning, the standard extended coverage perils, and vandalism and malicious mischief.   The limits of liability will be equal to one hundred percent (100%) of the insurable value of the Demolition Project, including items of labor and materials connected therewith, whether in or adjacent to the structure insured, and materials in place or to be used as part of the permanent construction. (b)   Comprehensive General Liability Insurance (including coverage for any construction on or about each lot, plot, parcel or part of the Selecto Flash Building Property) against claims for bodily injury or property damage occurring on, in or about the Selecto Flash Building Property and the adjoining streets, sidewalks and passageways, caused by or arising out of the Demolition Project and construction work that is the subject of this Agreement, in amounts not less than ten million ($10,000,000.00) dollars per occurrence with respect to any bodily injury and two million ($2,000,000.00) dollars per occurrence with respect to all claims for property damage;

-15- (c)   Worker's compensation insurance coverage in the amount of the full statutory liability of Interim Redeveloper; (d)   Such other insurance, in such amounts and against such risks, as is customarily maintained by Interim Redeveloper with respect to other similar properties owned or leased by it, including automobile insurance. Within ten (10) days from the Effective Date of this Agreement, Interim Redeveloper shall submit to Township proof of all applicable insurance. Thereafter, upon each anniversary date of this Agreement, Interim Redeveloper shall submit the aforementioned proofs of insurance, for the succeeding year.   The policies of insurance required to be maintained by Interim Redeveloper pursuant to Paragraph 2.10 shall name as the insured parties (except for worker's compensation insurance) the Interim Redeveloper, the Township, and their respective interests as they may appear, and shall be satisfactory to the Township.  2.11.   Indemnification . The Interim Redeveloper agrees to defend, indemnify and hold harmless the Township against, and Interim Redeveloper shall pay any and all liability, loss, cost, damage, claims, judgments or expenses, of any and all kinds or nature and however arising, imposed by law, which the Township may sustain, be subject to or be caused to incur by reason of any claim, suit or action based upon personal injury, death, or damage to property, whether real, personal or mixed, directly relating to the Interim Redeveloper’s activities in the Demolition Project or based upon or arising out of contracts entered into by the Interim Redeveloper which directly relate to the Demolition Project including but not limited to any and all claims by workmen, employees and agents of the Interim Redeveloper and unrelated third parties, which claims arise from   the   Demolition   Project   ,   or   any   other   activities   of   Interim   Redeveloper   within   the Redevelopment Area. It is mutually agreed by Interim Redeveloper and the Township that neither the Township, nor its Mayor, Council members, agents, servants or employees shall be liable in any event for any action performed under this Agreement and that Interim Redeveloper will indemnify the Township, its Mayor, Council members, officers, agents and employees harmless from any

-16- claim or suit in connection with the Interim Redeveloper’s obligations under this Agreement, except for any claim or suit alleging bodily injury (including death) or property damage to the extent that said claim arises out of the acts or omissions of the Township. The Interim Redeveloper, at its own cost and expense, shall defend any and all such claims, suits and actions which may be brought or asserted against the Township, its Mayor,   council members, officers, agents, servants or employees; but this provision shall not be deemed to relieve any insurance company which has issued a policy of insurance as may be provided for in this Agreement from its obligation to defend Interim Redeveloper, the Township and any other insured named in such policy of insurance in connection with claims, suits or actions covered by such policy. Any cost for reasonable attorneys’ fees in situations where it is necessary for the Township to engage its own attorneys, experts’ testimony, and all costs to defend the Township or   its Mayor and council members, officers, agents, servants, or employees shall be reimbursed to it by the Interim Redeveloper in connection with such indemnification claim.  ARTICLE III TOWNSHIP’S RESPONSIBILITIES  3.01.   Forbearance of Redevelopment Proposal.   Upon the Effective Date of this Agreement, the Township shall forbear the solicitation or negotiation of any Redevelopment Proposals for the Redevelopment Area for the Term of this Agreement.  3.02 .   Environmental Investigation and Remediation . The Interim Redeveloper agrees and understands that the Township shall continue to monitor and investigate the Selecto-Flash Building Property and any other parcel(s) within the Redevelopment Area in concert with the NJDEP and EPA consistent with all environmental regulations, programs and laws subject to the availability of grants or other similar monies.   The Township agrees that it will apply for and diligently pursue funding for the remediation of the Selecto-Flash Building Property under the Hazardous Discharge Site Remediation Program or other sources of funding available from the NJDEP.

-17-  3.03.   Cooperation and Access to the Selecto-Flash Property .   The Township shall permit representatives of the Interim Redeveloper to have access to any part of the Selecto-Flash Building Property as needed by the Interim Redeveloper to perform the Demolition Project and also to evaluate its potential proposal for future redevelopment. The Interim Redeveloper shall permit the representatives of the Township or their designees access to the Selecto-Flash Building Property at all reasonable times which any of them deems necessary for the purposes of assuring or ascertaining compliance with the terms of this Agreement, including, but not limited to, inspection of all work being performed in connection with the Demolition, unless such access constitutes a danger to those representatives or substantially compromises the Interim Redeveloper from completing the Demolition Project.   No compensation shall be payable nor shall any charge be made in any form by the Interim Redeveloper or the Township for the access provided herein.  3.04   Cooperation with Approvals .   The Township and Interim Redeveloper shall cooperate fully in the preparation and prosecution of any applications for governmental approvals required for the Demolition Project and any subsequent applications for development. The Interim Redeveloper shall provide to the Township copies of all applications, notices, and requests to any utility or government entity.  3.05.   Demolition Costs as Requirement from New Redeveloper . The Township agrees that the Interim Redeveloper shall be reimbursed any Demolition Costs it will incur under the terms set forth in Paragraph 2.05 herein up to the maximum amount set forth on the Demolition Proposal annexed hereto as Exhibit   “A . ”  ARTICLE IV  PROHIBITIONS AGAINST ASSIGNMENT  4.01.   Prohibition   Against   Assignments .   Both   the   Township   and   the   Interim Redeveloper agree that no rights under this Agreement shall be assigned or transferred to any third party, except that Interim Redeveloper may assign its rights under this Agreement to another Urban Renewal Entity in which Interim Redeveloper has an interest, without consent of the Township. Any

-18- proposed assignment to an entity in which Interim Redeveloper does not own a majority control or interest shall require Township approval, which shall not be unreasonably withheld, upon submission by the Interim Redeveloper of credible evidence that the assignee has sufficient experience, qualifications and capitalization to complete the Demolition Project and any subsequent redevelopment projects proposed for the Redevelopment Area.  ARTICLE V DEFAULT  5.01.   Events of Default .   Each of the following shall constitute an Event of Default (hereinafter referred to as an “Event of Default”): (a)   Interim Redeveloper’s Events of Default  (1)   If the Interim Redeveloper fails to meet any of the deadlines set forth in the Demolition Timeframe in Paragraph 2.06 as may or may not be extended by consent; or (2)   If   the   Interim   Redeveloper   interferes   or   inhibits   the   Townships,   the Township’s designees, the NJDEP, or the EPA from performing environmental monitoring, investigation or remediation on the Selecto-Flash Building Property; or (3)   If the Interim Redeveloper utilizes the Selecto-Flash Building Property or any other parcel within the Redevelopment Area in a manner inconsistent with the Agreement, the Redevelopment Plan, or any governing law or regulation; or (4)   If the Interim Redeveloper fails to obtain or maintain the required insurance as set forth in Paragraph 2.10 of this Agreement; or (5)   If the Interim Redeveloper be liquidated, or shall file a voluntary petition in bankruptcy or for an arrangement pursuant to the Bankruptcy Act or any similar law, federal or state, now or hereafter in effect, or shall make an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or shall admit in writing its inability to pay its debts as they become due, or shall suspend payment of its obligations, or shall take any action in furtherance of the foregoing; or if the Interim Redeveloper shall consent to the appointment of a receiver, or an answer proposing the adjudication of the

-19- Interim Redeveloper as a bankrupt or its reorganization pursuant to the Bankruptcy Act or any similar law, federal or state, now or hereafter in effect, shall be filed in and approved by a court of competent jurisdiction and the order approving the same shall not be vacated or set aside or stayed within ninety (90) days from entry thereof, or if the Interim Redeveloper shall consent to the filing of such petition or answer; (b)   Township’s Events of Default  (1)   If the Township interferes with the Interim Redeveloper’s ability to perform the Demolition Project consistent with the requirements of this Agreement; or (2)   If the Township designates another redeveloper during the Term; or (3)   If the Township fails to cooperate with the Interim Redeveloper’s efforts to prepare or prosecute any applications for governmental approvals required for the demolition or subsequent construction on the Selecto-Flash Building Property; or (4) If the Township fails to carry out its responsibilities with respect to investigation and remediation of contamination on the Selecto Flash Building Property as specified in Paragraph 2.07 herein provided sufficient grant monies are available; or (5) If the Township fails to carry out its contractual obligations set forth in Paragraph 2.08.  5.02.   Township’s Remedies .   Upon the occurrence of Interim Redeveloper’s Event of Default during the term of this Agreement, the Township shall provide written notice of the Event of Default to Interim Redeveloper, whereupon Interim Redeveloper shall have thirty (30) days to cure such Event of Default, provided however, that for so long as Interim Redeveloper is using commercially reasonable efforts to cure, if Interim Redeveloper is not capable of curing the subject Event of Default within such cure period, the Township and Interim Redeveloper shall agree on a time frame to cure.   If Interim Redeveloper is unable to cure within that agreed-upon timeframe, then the Township has the right upon ten (10) days’ notice to Interim Redeveloper to terminate this Agreement and demand that Interim Redeveloper cease all further actions related to the

-20- Redevelopment Area. The Township shall also have the right to recoup any damages associated with a default by the Interim Redeveloper by an action at law. Notwithstanding any termination of this Agreement pursuant to this subsection, Interim Redeveloper shall still be reimbursed its Demolition Costs pursuant to the provisions of Paragraphs 2.05 and 3.05 herein.  5.03.   Interim Redeveloper's Remedies.   Upon the occurrence of the Township’s Event of Default, the Interim Redeveloper shall shall provide written notice of the Event of Default to the Township, whereupon the Township shall have thirty (30) days to cure such Event of Default, provided however, that for so long as the Township is using commercially reasonable efforts to cure, if the Township is not capable of curing the subject Event of Default within such cure period, the Township and Interim Redeveloper shall agree on a time frame to cure.   If the Township is unable to cure within that agreed-upon timeframe, then the Interim Redeveloper has the right upon ten (10) days notice to the Township to terminate this Agreement and receive reimbursement from the Township of the Demolition Costs incurred to date.  5.04.   No Waiver of Rights and Remedies by Delay . Any delay by an aggrieved party in instituting or prosecuting any actions or proceedings or otherwise asserting its rights under this Agreement shall not operate as a waiver of such rights and shall not deprive the aggrieved party of or limit the aggrieved party’s rights in any way (it being the intent of this provision that the aggrieved party should not be constrained so as to avoid the risk of being deprived or limited in the exercise of the remedies provided herein by those concepts of waiver, laches, or otherwise to exercise such rights at a time when the aggrieved party may still hope otherwise to resolve the problems created by the default involved); nor shall any waiver in fact made by the aggrieved party with respect to any specific default by the defaulting party under this Agreement be considered or treated as a waiver of the rights of the aggrieved party with respect to any other defaults by the defaulting party under this Agreement or with respect to the particular default except to the extent specifically waived in writing.  5.05.   Rights and Remedies Cumulative . The rights and remedies of the parties to this Agreement, whether provided by law or by the terms of this Agreement, shall be cumulative, and

-21- the exercise by either party of any one or more of such remedies shall not preclude the exercise by it, at the same or different times, of any other such remedies for the same default or breach or of any of its remedies for any other default or breach by the other party. No waiver made by either such party with respect to the performance, or manner or time thereof, or any obligation of the other party or any condition to its own obligation under this Agreement shall be considered a waiver of any rights of the party making the waiver with respect to the particular obligation of the other party or condition to its own obligation beyond those expressly waived in writing and to the extent thereof, or a waiver in any respect in regard to any other rights of the party making the waiver or any other obligations of the other party.  ARTICLE VI REPRESENTATIONS  6.01.   Representations of Interim Redeveloper .   Interim Redeveloper represents and warrants to the Township that this Agreement has been duly authorized, executed and delivered by Interim Redeveloper and, on the Effective Date will constitute a legal, valid and binding obligation of Interim Redeveloper enforceable in accordance with its terms, except as enforceability may be limited by bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization or similar laws affecting creditor's rights generally and subject to the availability of equitable remedies; and the execution and delivery of this Agreement by Interim Redeveloper and consummation of the transactions contemplated hereby does not violate, conflict with or constitute a default under the provisions of any agreement, understanding or arrangement to which Interim Redeveloper is a party or by which it is bound or the Certificate of Incorporation, By-Laws, Certificate of Formation, Operating Agreement or Partnership Agreement of Interim Redeveloper, or any statute, rule, regulation, ordinance, order or decree in force as of the date hereof. Interim Redeveloper represents and warrants that it has obtained all necessary licenses, certifications and further represents that it will be qualified to do business in New Jersey on or after the Effective Date.

-22-  6.02.   Representation of the Township :   The Township represents and warrants to Interim Redeveloper that this Agreement has been duly authorized by virtue of a certain Resolution, executed and delivered by the Township and, on the Effective Date, will constitute a legal, valid and binding   obligation   of   the   Township   enforceable   in   accordance   with   its   terms,   except   as enforceability may be limited by bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization or similar laws affecting creditors' rights and subject to the availability of equitable remedies; and the execution and delivery of this Agreement by the Township and consummation of the transactions contemplated hereby does not violate, conflict with or constitute a default under the provisions of any agreement, understanding or arrangement to which the Township is a party or by which it is bound by any statute, rule, regulation, ordinance, order or decree in force as of the date hereof.  ARTICLE VII NOTICES AND DEMANDS  7.01.   Manner of Notice . A notice, demand, or other communication required under this Agreement by either party to the other shall be considered given and delivered if it is dispatched by registered or certified mail, overnight courier postage prepaid, return receipt requested, or delivered personally at the addresses listed below for each party. (a)   In the case of Township of West Orange : Mayor Robert D. Parisi Township of West Orange 66 Main Street West Orange, New Jersey 07052 With copy to: Richard D. Trenk, Esq. Trenk, DiPasquale, Della Fera & Sodono, P.C. 347 Mt. Pleasant Avenue West Orange, New Jersey 07052 (b)   In the case of the Alpert Group : Joseph Alpert 1 Parker Plaza Fort Lee, New Jersey 07024

-23- With copy to: William C. Sullivan, Jr., Esq. Scarinci Hollenbeck 1100 Valley Brook Avenue P.O. Box 790 Lyndhurst, New Jersey 07071 (c)   At such other addresses a party may, from time to time, designate in writing and mail to the other as provided herein.  ARTICLE VIII MISCELLANEOUS 8.01.   Force Majeure . It is agreed that the terms of this Agreement and any deadlines set forth herein, including but not limited to the Demolition Timetable set forth in Paragraph 2.05 shall be modified if completion of the Demolition or other compliance with this Agreement is prevented by an event of Force Majeure, as defined in the Definitions Section hereof. Where the completion of the Demolition or other compliance with this Agreement is prevented by an event of Force Majeure, the relevant term(s) of this Agreement and/or any deadline shall be modified/extended for the period of delay caused thereby, provided that the Interim Redeveloper who seeks the benefit of this provision on force majeure shall, within ten (10) days after the beginning of any such enforced delay, have notified the Township in writing, and of the cause or causes thereof, and therein has requested a modification of the term and/or an extension for the period of the enforced delay. In addition to the examples of Force Majeure set forth in the Definitions Section hereof, compliance with   municipal   laws   regulating   land   use   and   rehabilitation   and/or   construction,   any   legal requirements under any applicable environmental laws, as well as known NJDEP clearances, approvals, or permits typical of the development process and referred to in this Agreement shall be construed to be an event(s) of Force Majeure.  8.02.   Non   Default   Termination .   The   Interim   Redeveloper   acknowledges   and understands that the Township’s title to the Selecto-Flash Building Property arose from the Final Judgment issued by the Superior Court of New Jersey following the Township’s In Rem Tax

-24- Foreclosure. The Interim Redeveloper further acknowledges and understands that the previous title owner to the Selecto-Flash Building Property may seek to redeem its interest in the Selecto-Flash Building Property or seek to amend the Final Judgment pursuant to New Jersey Court Rule 4:50-1 up to one year from the date of the Final Judgment.   In the event that the previous title owner applies to the Superior Court of New Jersey to redeem its interest or amend the Final Judgment, the Township will provide notice to the Interim Redeveloper, upon which notice the Interim Redeveloper may elect to terminate this Agreement. In that event, to the extent that the Township is reimbursed or compensated in any manner by the prior title owner of   the Selecto-Flash Property, then the Township will reimburse Interim Redeveloper for the Demolition Costs incurred by Interim Redeveloper. 8.03.   Right of Entry For Utility Service . The Township reserves for itself and any public utility company, as may be appropriate, the unqualified right to enter upon the Selecto-Flash Building Property at any reasonable time for the purpose of reconstructing, maintaining, repairing, or servicing the public utilities located within the Selecto-Flash Building Property boundary lines provided written notice is given to Interim Redeveloper prior to entry. Such written notice shall not be required in the case of an emergency occurring on or around the Redevelopment Area, the conditions of which would require immediate or emergency entry.  8.04.   Interim Redeveloper Not To Block or Obscure Utility Easements . The Interim Redeveloper shall obscure or block access to any easement for public utilities described or referred to in Paragraph 8.03 herein, unless such blockage is approved by the Township. If approval for such blockage is requested by the Interim Redeveloper, the Township shall use its best efforts to assure that such approval shall not be withheld unreasonably.  8.05.   Maintenance and Security of Selecto Flash Building Property . The Interim Redeveloper shall be responsible for the maintenance and security of the Selecto-Flash Building Property subject to this Agreement and shall continue to do so until such time as Interim Redeveloper completes the Demolition pursuant to Paragraph 2.01.   Interim Redeveloper shall

-25- keep the Selecto-Flash Building Property safe, clean and kept so that any garbage or debris is promptly collected and disposed of.  8.06.   Equal Employment Opportunity . The Interim Redeveloper agrees that during the Demolition: (a)   The Interim Redeveloper will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, religion, sex, or national origin. The Interim Redeveloper will take affirmative action to ensure that applicants are employed, and that employees are treated during employment, without regard to their race, color, religion, sex or national origin. Such action shall include, but not be limited to, the following: employment, upgrading, demotion, or transfer, recruitment or recruitment advertising, layoff or termination; rates of pay or other forms of compensation; and selection for training, including apprenticeship. The Interim Redeveloper agrees to post in conspicuous places, available to employees and applicants for employment, notices setting forth the provisions of this nondiscrimination clause and any such notices provided by the Township which are consistent therewith; (b)   The Interim Redeveloper will, in all solicitations or advertisements for employees placed by or on behalf of the Interim Redeveloper, state that all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex or national origin; (c)   The Interim Redeveloper will send to each labor union or representative of workers with which the Interim Redeveloper has a collective bargaining agreement or other contract or understanding, a notice, advising the labor union workers' representative of the Interim Redeveloper's commitments under Section 202 of Executive Order 11246 of September 24, 1965 as amended and modified, and shall post copies of said notice in conspicuous places available to employees and applicants for employment; (d)   The Interim Redeveloper will comply with all provisions of Executive Order No. 11246, 30 Fed. Reg. 12319 (1965), and all rules, regulations, and relevant orders of the Secretary of Labor;

-26- (e)   Subcontractors and suppliers to the Project shall to the extent reasonably possible include qualified and certified minority enterprises; and (f)   To the maximum extent possible, the obligations in this Section shall be binding on all contractors and subcontractors to the extent that any work is done by any contractor or subcontractor, and any contract entered into by the Interim Redeveloper shall so provide.  8.07.   Entire Agreement . This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement of the parties and supersedes the prior or contemporaneous writings, discussions, or agreements between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may not be modified, or amended except by a written agreement specifically referring to this Agreement signed by all the parties hereto.  8.08.   Titles of Articles and Paragraphs/Sections/Headings . Any titles of the several Parts and Sections of the Agreement are inserted for convenience of reference only and shall be disregarded in construing or interpreting any of its provisions. The Section headings contained in this Agreement are inserted for reference purposes only and shall be given no weight in the construction of this Agreement. None of the headings or titles of Articles and Sections are intended to limit or define the contents of the Sections and Articles.  8.09.   Counterparts . This Agreement is executed in several counterparts, each of which shall constitute one and the same instrument.  8.10.   Severability . If any provision of this Agreement shall be determined to be invalid or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, such determination shall not affect the remaining provisions of this Agreement, all of which shall remain in full force and effect.  8.11   Governing Law.   This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New Jersey and any legal or remedial action taken hereunder shall be in the Superior Court of the State of New Jersey; Essex County.  8.12   WAIVER   OF   JURY.   EACH PARTY TO THIS AGREEMENT HEREBY EXPRESSLY WAIVES ANY RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY OF ANY CLAIM, DEMAND, ACTION OR CAUSE OF ACTION (1) ARISING THIS AGREEMENT OR ANY OTHER
-i-  EXHIBIT "A" PROPOSAL

AGREEMENT WITH MATRIX FOR PRELIMINARY ASSESSMENT AND SITE IMP.

pages.text: 75-17  March 7, 2017  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   Resolution   121-16,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the  “ Township ” )   appointed   Matrix   New   World   ( “ Matrix ” )   to   conduct   a   Preliminary   Assessment   and  Site   Investigation   as   to   the   property   located   at   18   Central   Avenue,   Block   9,   Lot   3 6 ,   and   4  Tompkins   Street,   Block   7,   Lot   22   on   the   Tax   Map   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the  “ Selecto-Flash Properties ” ); and  WHEREAS,   on   August   9,   2016,   the   Township   passed   resolution   189-16,   which  authorized   Matrix   to   apply   for   grant   funds   from   the   New   Jersey   Department   of   Environmental  Protection/New   Jersey   Economic   Development   Authority   (the   “ DEP ” )   Hazardous   Discharge   Site  Remediation   Funds   (the   “ HDSRF ” )   Program   for   the   purpose   of   funding   the   cost   for   the  Preliminary Assessment and Site Investigation   on the Selecto-Flash Properties ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   received   notice   that   the   application   for   grant   funds   from   the  HDSRF   were   approved   in   the   amount   of   $77,237.90   and   requires   various   finalized   documents,  including   an   executed   agreement   with   Matrix   for   performance   of   the   Preliminary   Assessment  and Site Investigation   on the Selecto-Flash Properties ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   costs   for   the   Preliminary   Assessment   and   Site   Investigation   on   the  Selecto-Flash   Properties   are   to   be   paid   from   the   $77,237.90   grant   funds   to   be   received   from   the  HDSRF.  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Mayor   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the  Agreement   to   Conduct   Preliminary   Assessment   and   Site   Investigation ,   annexed   hereto   as  Exhibit   “ A; ”   and be it further

RESOLVED ,   that   the   Township   Clerk   is   authorized   to   attest   to   the   Mayor ’ s   signature;  and be it further  RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   Office  for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Joe Krakoviak  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: March 7, 2017  I hereby ce rtify funds are available from Account No.  John Gross,   Chief Financial Officer  4839-2408-5828, v. 1
Exhibit   “A”

1  AGREEMENT TO CONDUCT PRELIMINARY ASSESSMENT AND SITE INVESTIGATION THIS   AGREEMENT   FOR   RETENTION   TO   CONDUCT   A   PRELIMINARY ASSESSMENT AND SITE INVESTIGATION   (this “Agreement”), made as of the ____ day of March, 2017, by and between   THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE , (the “Township”) and   MATRIX NEW WORLD   (“Matrix”).  W I T N E S S E T H : WHEREAS,   the Township has designated Matrix as its Licensed Site Remediation Professional ("LSRP") for various properties within   an   area   designated the   Central   Valley Brownsfield Development Area (the “BDA”) which includes the properties located at 18 Central Avenue, Block 9, Lot 36 on the Tax Map of the Township of West Orange and 4 Tompkins Street, Block 7, Lot 22 on the Tax Map of the Township of West Orange (collectively   the “ Selecto-Flash Property”); and  WHEREAS ,   Matrix has submitted a grant application on behalf of the Township to the Hazardous Discharge Site Remediation Fund Grant (the “HDSRF”) through the Department of Environmental Protection (the “DEP”) and the New Jersey Economic Development Authority (the “EDA”); and  WHEREAS,   Matrix's submission to the HDSRF included   a REVISED Preliminary Assessment and Site Investigation Scope of Work (the “Scope of Work”), annexed hereto as Exhibit   “A;”   and  WHEREAS , Matrix’s submission to the HDSRF also included a Cost Estimate to perform a Preliminary Assessment and Site Investigation Scope of Work, annexed hereto as Exhibit   “B”   totaling   $77,237.90 ; and

2  WHEREAS , by letter dated February 17, 2017, the EDA provided a Notice of Approval to the Township advising that it had approved the Township’s application for a grant from the HDSRF up to   $77,237.90 ; and  NOW, THEREFORE,   for and in consideration of the covenants and agreements hereinafter set forth and other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which is hereby acknowledged, and the mutual promises herein made, it is agreed as follows:  1.   SCOPE OF WORK.   Matrix agrees to perform a Preliminary Assessment and Site Investigation of the Selecto-Flash Property consistent with the Scope of Work, annexed hereto as Exhibit   “A.” 2.   COST.   The Township agrees to pay Matrix for services performed under this Agreement pursuant to the Cost Estimate drafted by Matrix and submitted to the HDSRF, annexed hereto as Exhibit   “B.”   Matrix acknowledges and agrees its payments shall be paid from the funds received by the HDSRF grant by the Township. Matrix acknowledges and agrees that any payments beyond those enumerated in the Cost Estimate will only be paid to the extent that the HDSRF approves or authorizes additional funds beyond the $77,238 approved by the EDA in its Notice of Approval dated February 17, 2017.  3.   BILLING AND PAYMENTS.   Matrix agrees to provide monthly invoices to the Township no later than the final business day of each month. Each invoice will contain detailed entries showing the tasks performed during that month.   In addition, to the monthly totals for work performed by Matrix, each invoice will also have an entry showing the running balance of totals for work from the commencement of Matrix’s performance of the Preliminary Assessment and Site Investigation. Matrix will submit a copy of the invoice to the following:

3 Via Email - rtrenk@trenklawfirm.com Richard D. Trenk, Esq. Trenk, DiPasquale, Della Fera and Sodono, P.C. 347 Mt. Pleasant Avenue, Suite 300 West Orange, NJ 07052 Via Email - JGross@westorange.org John Gross Chief Financial Officer Township of West Orange 66 Main Street West Orange, NJ 07052 Via Email - Llepore@westorange.org Leonard Lepore, P.E. Municipal Engineer/Director of Public Works Township of West Orange 25 Lakeside Avenue West Orange, NJ 07052  5.   COMPLIANCE WITH ALL HDSRF REQUIREMENTS.   Matrix agrees that it will comply with all requirements set forth by the HDSRF, including, but not limited to, the completion and submission of a Public Entity Project Summary to be submitted no later than December 31 of each year as required by the HDSRF.  6.   TOWNSHIP COUNCIL APPROVAL.   The parties’ obligations set forth in this Agreement are expressly subject to approval by the Township Council for the Township of West Orange (the “Township Council”)   to   be manifested in   the   adoption   of a resolution authorizing the execution of this Agreement.  7.   ENTIRE AGREEMENT.   This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties. If any of the terms of this Agreement conflict with any previous agreement or   contract   between   the   parties,   the   terms   of   this   Agreement   prevail.   No   variation   or modifications of or amendments to the terms of this Agreement shall be binding unless reduced to writing and signed by the parties hereto.

4  8.   BINDING EFFECT.   This Agreement shall be binding on and shall inure to the benefit of the Township and Matrix and their respective successors and assigns.  9.   CONSTRUCTION.   The laws of the State of New Jersey shall govern the interpretation, construction and performance of this Agreement.  10.   CAPTIONS.   The captions preceding the paragraphs of this Agreement are intended only as a matter of convenience for reference and in no way define, limit or describe the scope of this Agreement or the intent of any provisions hereof.  11.   SEVERABILITY.   The   terms, conditions, covenants and   provisions of   this Agreement shall be deemed to be severable. If any clause or provision herein contained shall be determined to be invalid or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction or by operation of applicable law, the same shall be deemed to be severable and shall not affect the validity of any other clause or provision of this Agreement and such other clauses and provisions shall remain in full force and effect.  12.   GENDER.   As used in this Agreement, the masculine gender shall include the feminine or neuter genders, the neuter gender shall include the masculine or feminine genders, the   singular   shall   include   the   plural   and   the   plural   shall   include   the   singular,   wherever appropriate to the context.  13.   MISCELLANEOUS.  A.   Each   person   signing   this   Agreement   warrants   that   he/she   has   fully authority to execute this Agreement and bind the party on whose behalf he/she has signed to the terms hereof. B.   This Agreement may be executed in two or more counterparts, and when all counterparts have been executed, each executed counterpart will have the force and effect of
Exhibit   “A”

REVISED   Preliminary   Assessment   and   Site   Investigation  Scope   of   Work 1.0   INTRODUCTION Selecto   Flash,   Inc.   Site  18   Central   Avenue   &   4   Tompkins   Street West   Orange,   Essex County, New   Jersey NJDEP   PI   #027054  August   5, 2016 The following revised scope   of   work has been prepared   by   the Township   of   West Orange as part   of   the request   for   a   Brownfield   Development   Area   (BOA)   grant   from   the   Hazardous   Discharge   Site  Remediation Fund (HDSRF).   Funding is being requested to complete a   Preliminary   Assessment   (P   A) and   Site   Investigation   (SI)   of   the   Selecto   Flash, Inc.   Site   (Selecto Flash), with parcels located at 18 Central Avenue and 4 Tompkins   Street   in West   Orange,   Essex County,   New   Jersey. The   Site   is part   of   the Orange West/Orange Central   Valley   BOA.   The   Site   is approximately 1.5 acres in size and is located in a mixed industrial, commercial, and residential area   of   West   Orange   Township. The overall goal   of   the project is to obtain sufficient information on the potential contamination issues to evaluate the development options for the   Site.   This scope   of   work and attached cost estimate is being submitted to the New Jersey Department   of   Environmental   Protection   (NJDEP)   for their approval to request funding from the Hazardous Discharge   Site   Remediation Fund   (HDSRF)   to begin environmental investigations at the   Selecto   Flash   Site.   The   Site   is identified   by   the   NJDEP   as   PI   No.   027054   and Case Nos. E86935 and 95-10-18-1521-31.   These case numbers appear to be related to a previous sale   of   the property under the former Environmental Cleanup Responsibility Act (ECRA) and a discharge reported for the release from an out-of-service underground storage   tank   (UST).   Available on-line records indicate a General Information Notice (GIN) was submitted in 1986 followed   by   the submission   of   a   PA   Report in 1987 to the   NJDEP   under the former ECRA program. The scope   of   this project includes the completion   of   a   PA   to identify any potential areas   of   concern  (AOCs)   present at the property known as Block 9; Lot 36 (18 Central Ave.) and Block   7;   Lot 22   (4 Tompkins   St.)   and the completion   of   a   SI   for any   AOCs   identified during the   PA.   The   PA   and   SI   are scoped to comply   with   the   NJDEP   Technical Requirements for   Site   Remediation (TRSR), the   Site  Remediation   Reform   Act   (SRRA),   the   Administrative   Requirements   for   the   Remediation   of Contaminated   Sites   (ARRCS), Underground   Storage   Tanks,   and the   NJDEP's   applicable guidance documents.   The information obtained from the   PA   will be used to determine which   SI   activities are warranted. Based on available information, it appears that the property includes a, two-story building and paved parking area. 2.0   TECHNICAL   APPROACH The Township   of   West Orange is acquiring the properties at   18   Central Avenue (Block 9; Lot 36) and 4 Tompkins Street (Block 7; Lot 22) in West Orange,   New   Jersey through foreclosure.   The scope   of   work for this   PA   and   SI   is prepared in accordance with the TRSR. The objective   of   this project is to   identifY   any areas on the site where environmental conditions are present   as   a result   of   past practices, and to evaluate these areas with respect   to   site development. The first step will   be   to complete a   PA   of   the properties known   as   Block 9; Lot   36   (18 Central Avenue) and 4 Tompkins   Street   (Block 7; Lot 22).   The   PA   will be based primarily   on   readily available information regarding environmental   incidents   and   historical   information.   This   phase   uses   only non-intrusive  M   :\M atrix   IDS   I1 obs\10560101 \Repo   rts   \HDS RF   G rant\Selc:<:toF   l ash   RDSRF   Grant   A pp licat io n'REVISED_HDS RF   Application_   8- 2-16\Finai_REVISED _ HDSRF _SclectoFluh_pA -S   I   SOW _g .  5-l6'REV _ HDS RF   Selectnl'l uh   _P A-   Sl   SO W _8· 5·16.doc

HDSRF   Application  Selecto   Flash, Inc. Page 2   of8 methods   to   identify   any   environmental conditions that may exist at the   Site.   According to the NJDEP TRSR, the intent   of   the   PA   is   to   identify the presence   of   any potentially contaminated   AOCs   that require further investigation.   Based   on   the review   of   NJDEP   Data Miner environmental records, at least one  AOC,   a   10,000-gallon,   No, 4 heating oil   UST   is associated with the   Selecto   Flash   Site.   The   UST   status is reported   to   be   out-of-service   and   the   UST   registration   is   expired.   A   Licensed   Site   Remediation Professional   (LSRP)   will   be   retained   by   the Township   of   West Orange for the   Selecto   Flash   Site.  Those areas where the P   A determines the likelihood   of   unacceptable environmental conditions   to   be   high will   be   addressed   in   the   SI   phase.   During the site investigation phase, at a minimum, a subsurface soil investigation will   be   conducted to determine   if   historical operations have impacted the site soil. The results   of   this   Scope   of   Work will provide the Township   of   West Orange with the following information:  •   what potential   AOCs   exist on this   Site;  •   if   the potential for soil and groundwater contamination exists;  •   type   of   soil and groundwater contamination,   if   any, that may   be   present; and  •   if   the potential for vapor intrusion exists These goals will   be   accomplished by:  •   conducting a PA   of   this property; including a site walk and historical file reviews;  •   preparing   PA   Report and accompanying   NJDEP   form   to   be   submitted to the NJDEP;  •   conducting   SI   activities   to   evaluate the soil conditions and groundwater beneath the   Site   (if necessary)   in   the   AOCs   identified;  •   preparing a   SI   Report to   be   submitted to the NJDEP with recommendations for any further investigations. The following describes the technical approach that will   be   implemented to accomplish the above goals.  2.1   PRELIMINARY   ASSESSMENT   TASKS  The objective   of   a PA is to identify potential environmental   AOCs   at a   Site.   The   NJDEP   defines   an environmental   AOC   as   "any   existing or former location where hazardous substances, hazardous wastes, or   pollutants are   or   were known   or   suspected to have been discharged, generated, manufactured, refined, transported, stored, handled, treated, disposed,   or   where hazardous substances, hazardous wastes,   or pollutants have   or   may have   migrated".   The   TRSR   (N.J.A.C. 7:26E-1.8) and the   Preliminary   Assessment guidance document provides a list   of   potential types   of   AOCs,   which   may   include storage tanks, sumps, dumpsters, drainage systems, transformers, spill areas, compressor vent discharges, boiler rooms, and hazardous material storage areas. The PA will   be   completed   in   accordance with the   NJDEP   TRSR   and the   NJDEP   Preliminary   Assessment guidance document.   As   required   by   the NJDEP, the   PA   effort will include compilation   of   required information, review   of   this information, and the preparation   of   the PA report. Based on our knowledge   of the   NJDEP   regulatory framework,   the   following tasks will   be   conducted. HDSRF   Application  Selecto   Flash, Inc.  Site Visit  Page   3   of   8 A site visit will   be   conducted to gain an understanding   of   current site conditions.   The   site visit will include a site walk to identify   any   potential   AOCs,   a visual inspection   of   building/structures and exterior areas, and a review   of   available plant and/or property files dealing with current and former environmental conditions.   The   inspection team will compile the site information and prepare a summary   of   findings. These inspections will   be   limited to those site features which are readily accessible and visible.   No sampling   or   testing is included   under   this task.  Review   of   Commercially Available Environmental Databases  A database report will   be   obtained and reviewed from one   of   the recognized database report providers to identify any historical environmental   concerns relating   to   the   site,   as   well   as adjacent properties. Information   may   be   located   in   one   or   more   of   the following databases:  •   USEPA   National   Priorities   List Database.  •   USEP A   Resource   Conservation   and   Recovery   Act   (RCRA)   Information   System   (RCRIS)  Database.  •   USEP A   Comprehensive Environmental   Response,   Compensation,   and   Liability   Information  System   (CERCLIS)   Database.  •   RCRA Corrective Action Activity   (CORRACTS)   Database.  •   Leaking   Underground   Storage   Tanks   (LUST)   Site   Database.  •   Solid   Waste Facilities/Landfill   Sites   Database.  •   Registered   Underground   Storage   Tanks   (UST)   Site   Database.  •   Facility Index   System   Database.  •   Hazardous Materials Information Reporting   System   Database.  •   State   Ha zardous Waste   Site   Database.  •   CERCLA   No   Further Remedial Action   Planned   (NFRAP) Database.  •   Major Facilities Database.  •   Toxic Chemical Release Inventory   System   Database.  •   Toxic   Substances   Control   Act   (TSCA)   Database. Copies   of   any   available aerial photographs   of   the   Site   will also   be   obtained and reviewed.   These sources are expected to provide historical site development information and historic site conditions.  Review   of   NJDEP   Files  A request will   be   submitted to the   NJDEP   to review files regarding   the   former   Selecto   Flash property located   at   18   Central Avenue   and   4 Tompkins   Street,   West   Orange,   New   Jersey.   Available site records and files will   be   reviewed that are related to environmental   or   health and safety compliance issues   to identify   any   potential past situations that have occurred that indicate site contamination.   Any   ar eas   of potential contamination identified during the records review will   be   described   on   drawings   of   the site. In addition, a review   of   the operating   and   ownership history will   be   performed as well   as   UST   registration files and Community Right to Know Surveys. A review   of   existing   ECRA   and/or Industrial   Site   Recovery Act   (ISRA)   files will also   be   completed. Review   of Local   Agency   Files  Page4   of8 Local government agencies will   be   contacted to determine whether records   of   violations   or   other records exist that may   be   useful   in   identifying areas   of   concern.   Information concerning violations   of   health   or fire codes can provide important information regarding potential areas   of   envirorunental concern.  PA   Report   Preparation  A PA report will   be   completed   in   conformance with NJDEP's Technical Requirements, summarizing the various investigations and presenting a general assessment   of   the site conditions.   The PA Report will contain all the information obtained from the historical data searches, the regulatory file review, and the site inspection. The   PA   Report will also contain recommendations for each   AOC   identified. Following a review   of   the Draft Report   by   the Township   of   West   Orange,   comments will   be   incorporated to prepare a Final Preliminary Assessment Report that will be submitted to NJDEP. During this phase   of   the investigation,   no   environmental samples will   be   collected and no other intrusive activities will   be   conducted as part   of   the PA.  2.2   SITE   INVESTIGATION TASKS  The objective   of   a   SI   is to evaluate potential environmental   AOCs   identified during the   PA   that   may   have impacted site soils and/or groundwater.   The proposed scope   of   work and technical approach for the investigation has been developed in accordance with the   TRSR   N.J.A.C. 7:26E, the NJDEP   Field Sampling Procedures Manual   (August   2005),   and applicable NJDEP guidance documents. Information available for the former   Selecto   Flash property located at   18   Central A   venue, West Orange indicates the property consists of one, two-story building and paved parking area.   NJD   EP records available on-line indicate the presence   of   one   10,000-gallon,   cathodically protected steel, single-walled, No. 4 heating oil,   UST.   The   UST   status is listed as empty and out   of   service in 1979 which indicates the  UST   is   currently out   of   compliance.   The   UST   registration was listed as expired in   2002.   No   further information was available. Information available for the former   Selecto   Flash property located at 4 Tompkins   Street,   We   st   Orange  indicates it   is   a vacant, 0.16-acre parcel that appears   to   be   paved and used for parking. No NJDEP online information is available for the property located   at   4 Tompkins   Street,   West Orange, New Jersey.  Quality   Assurance   Project   Plan   (QAPP)  A QAPP and a Health and   Safety   Plan   (HASP)   will   be   prepared prior to the commencement   of   the   SI  field activities. The QAPP will   be   prepared   in   accordance with requirements   in   the   TRSR   and following the NJDEP Quality Assurance Project Plan Technical Guidance. The site-specific   HASP   will   be   prepared in   accordance with 29   CFR Parts   1910   and 1926   of   the Federal Occupational   Safety   and Health Administration   (OSHA)   regulations.
